## Pull request overview

Adds a (POC) diversification/selection mechanism to vector retrieval by introducing an MMR-based post-processing selector and plumbing a new `Selection`/`selector` parameter through gRPC, cluster API payloads, sharding, DB search, and vector index interfaces.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `searchparams.Selection` (+ gRPC `Selection` proto) and threads it through search request parsing, sharding/remote search APIs, DB/shard search paths, and vector index interfaces.
- Adds an MMR selector implementation (`adapters/repos/db/vector/selection`) and integrates it into HNSW search results post-processing.
- Updates tests and cluster API payload compatibility to carry the new selector field.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 53 out of 54 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| usecases/sharding/remote_index_incoming.go | Adds `selector` param to incoming shard search interface/call. |

| usecases/classification/integrationtest/fakes_for_integration_test.go | Updates fake remote client signature to include `selector`. |
| grpc/proto/v1/base_search.proto | Adds `Selection` message and `selection` fields to near/hybrid messages. |
| grpc/generated/protocol/v1/base_search.pb.go | Regenerates Go proto bindings for new `Selection` message/fields. |
| entities/searchparams/retrieval.go | Introduces `Selection`/`SelectionMMR` types in search params. |
| entities/dto/dto.go | Adds `Selector` field to `dto.GetParams`. |
| entities/aggregation/params.go | Adds `Selection` to aggregation params payload. |

title: fix(reranker-cohere): upgrade rerank endpoint from v1 to v2 API
body:
Changed the hardcoded Cohere rerank API path from `/v1/rerank` to `/v2/rerank`.

## Reason

- Azure AI Foundry only supports Cohere's v2 API, v1 is deprecated there
- Cohere's v2 is the current standard
- This unblocks Azure users without any config changes on their end

## Test plan

- [x] `go test ./modules/reranker-cohere/...` passes
- [ ] Manual verify against a real Cohere v2 endpoint (or Azure)

title: Implements AUTHENTICATION_OIDC_INSECURE_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY
body:
### WARNING:

This should never be enabled in production and should only be used for testing / research / development purposes.

## Motivation

Weaviate's OIDC client has no way to skip TLS certificate verification when connecting to the OIDC provider. This blocks deployments where the OIDC server uses a self-signed or internal CA certificate and the operator can't (or doesn't want to) supply the full certificate PEM via `AUTHENTICATION_OIDC_CERTIFICATE` — for example, dev/staging environments, air-gapped setups, or OIDC servers behind a corporate proxy with certificate interception.

## Approach

A new boolean env var `AUTHENTICATION_OIDC_INSECURE_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY=true` is exposed. When set, the OIDC HTTP client is built with `tls.Config.InsecureSkipVerify = true`.

The existing TLS client construction was refactored to centralise all TLS config in a single `buildHTTPClient()`. Previously, a custom HTTP client was only created when a certificate was configured; now it's also created when `SkipTLSVerify` is true. The two options compose — setting both loads the cert pool **and** disables verification (a warning is logged in that case since `RootCAs` is effectively ignored when `InsecureSkipVerify=true`).

The field uses the same `*runtime.DynamicValue[bool]` pattern as all other OIDC config fields, so it can also be toggled at runtime via the YAML runtime config file without a restart. The field is wired into `WeaviateRuntimeConfig` as `OIDCSkipTLSVerify` and synced in `configure_api.go` alongside all other OIDC runtime fields.

The OIDC struct's YAML/JSON config key uses `insecure_skip_tls_verify` to carry the same security-signal prefix as the env var. Note that `loadCertPool` fetches certificate URLs using the default `http.Client` — if the cert URL is itself behind a self-signed endpoint, the fetch will fail even with `SkipTLSVerify=true` (this is a pre-existing chicken-and-egg constraint, now documented).

## Key areas for review

- [`middleware.go:buildHTTPClient`](https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/blob/4447cf1e112455473e834967a7aab2adfca9afa5/usecases/auth/authentication/oidc/middleware.go) — verify `InsecureSkipVerify` is only applied when explicitly opted in and the `#nosec G402` annotation is appropriate
- [`middleware.go:Init`](https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/blob/4447cf1e112455473e834967a7aab2adfca9afa5/usecases/auth/authentication/oidc/middleware.go) — the condition `Certificate != "" || SkipTLSVerify` that decides whether to inject a custom HTTP client into the OIDC context
- [`configure_api.go`](https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/blob/4447cf1e112455473e834967a7aab2adfca9afa5/adapters/handlers/rest/configure_api.go) — `OIDCSkipTLSVerify` is now wired into the runtime config block alongside all other OIDC fields
- [`environment.go`](https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/blob/4447cf1e112455473e834967a7aab2adfca9afa5/usecases/config/environment.go) — env var parsing is gated inside the `AUTHENTICATION_OIDC_ENABLED` block

## Risks and mitigations

- **Disabling TLS verification in production**: requires explicit opt-in; a `Warn`-level log is emitted on every startup when the flag is set, making it visible in logs and alerting
- **`InsecureSkipVerify` triggers gosec G402**: suppressed with `#nosec G402` and an inline comment referencing the opt-in env var

## Testing

- **Unit** (`Test_Middleware_SkipTLSVerify_BuildsInsecureClient`): verifies `http.Transport.TLSClientConfig` directly for three cases — skip-only, cert-only, and both together
- **Integration** (`Test_Middleware_SkipTLSVerify_WithTLSServer`): spins up a real OIDC server via `httptest.NewTLSServer` (self-signed cert); confirms `Init()` fails without the flag and succeeds with `SkipTLSVerify=true`, including full token validation through `ValidateAndExtract`
- **Config** (`TestEnvironmentAuthentication`): asserts the new field is parsed correctly — both the default `false` case and the `true` case

Docs will follow approval.

### Review checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:
- [x] All new code is covered by tests where it is reasonable.
- [ ] Performance tests have been run or not necessary.

commits.message:
Fix OIDC SkipTLSVerify wiring and clean up related code

Wire OIDCSkipTLSVerify into runtime config in configure_api.go — it was defined but never synced, causing dynamic config updates to use a stale value
Remove dead useCertificate() wrapper (private method with no external callers); update tests to call buildHTTPClient() directly
Warn when both Certificate and SkipTLSVerify are set simultaneously, since RootCAs is effectively ignored when InsecureSkipVerify=true
Document the loadCertPool limitation: HTTP cert URL fetches use the default http.Client and will fail if the cert URL is behind a self-signed endpoint
Rename OIDC struct yaml/json tags from skip_tls_verify to insecure_skip_tls_verify to match the security-signal naming convention
Change env var from INSECURE_SKIP_OIDC_TLS_VERIFY to AUTHENTICATION_OIDC_INSECURE_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY
make yaml config same with env var name
Make certificate and insecure_skip_tls_verify mutually exclusive with test

Restore log line and fix test helper after buildHTTPClient refactor

- Restore the "configured OIDC client with custom certificate" log line
  in buildHTTPClient() that was lost during the refactor
- Update loadCertPool godoc to reflect that Certificate and SkipTLSVerify
  are now mutually exclusive (the stale chicken-and-egg caveat no longer applies)
- Fix verifyLogs test helper in Test_Middleware_CertificateDownload to search
  for the specific "custom certificate is valid" entry rather than asserting
  every log entry matches, which broke once buildHTTPClient also emits a log

title: test: improve async deletion verification in crash recovery test
body:
### What's being changed:

This pull request improves the reliability of the crash recovery test by ensuring that the test waits for asynchronous cleanup to complete after a node restart. The main change is to use `require.Eventually` to poll for the removal of `.deleteme` files, rather than checking immediately.

Test reliability improvement:

* Updated the `TestAsyncDeletion_CrashRecovery` test in `crash_recovery_test.go` to use `require.Eventually` for checking that all `.deleteme` files are removed after restart, accounting for the asynchronous nature of the cleanup process.

title: Export collection: Add point in time exports
body:
### What's being changed:

This switches export collection to create snaphots of the shards that are about to be exported during the export call. This brings several improvements:
- We have point-in-time exports (where the shard state that is exported is the state during the call to start the backup)
- We do not block shard (un-) loading during the exports. All exports happen from the snapshot and the normal shard is not touched anymore

On a lower level this PR adds:
- a snapshot bucket that is read only and all the helpers around it
- changes the export to use these snapshot buckets
- in the 2pc, the prepare call to a node will kick off the (concurrent) snapshot creation. The commit call will block until all snapshots have been created


### Review checklist

## Pull request overview This PR introduces LSMKV bucket snapshots by hard-linking immutable segment files into a dedicated snapshot directory, and adds support for opening those snapshots as read-only buckets. **Changes:** - Add `Bucket.CreateSnapshot` and `NewSnapshotBucket` to create/open snapshot-backed buckets. - Add a `WithReadOnly` bucket option and enforce read-only behavior in write paths via `getActiveMemtableForWrite`/`FlushAndSwitch`. - Add snapshot-related tests and clean up the index-level snapshots root directory on index creation. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | adapters/repos/db/lsmkv/bucket_snapshot.go | Implements snapshot creation via hardlinks and snapshot bucket opening. | | adapters/repos/db/lsmkv/bucket_snapshot_test.go | Adds tests for snapshot creation, isolation, and read-only behavior. | | adapters/repos/db/lsmkv/bucket.go | Introduces `ErrReadOnly`, `readOnly` flag, snapshot-dir guard, and propagates write errors. | | adapters/repos/db/lsmkv/bucket_options.go | Adds `WithReadOnly` option. | | adapters/repos/db/lsmkv/bucket_roaring_set.go | Updates write methods to handle `getActiveMemtableForWrite` error. | | adapters/repos/db/lsmkv/bucket_roaring_set_range.go | Updates write methods to handle `getActiveMemtableForWrite` error. | | adapters/repos/db/lsmkv/bucket_test.go | Updates tests for new `getActiveMemtableForWrite` signature. | | adapters/repos/db/index.go | Adds snapshots root path helper and removes snapshots root during index creation. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

## Pull request overview

This PR changes collection export to use point-in-time shard snapshots (hard-linked LSM bucket snapshots) created during the 2PC Prepare phase, so the subsequent scan/upload reads a frozen view without blocking shard load/unload.

**Changes:**
- Add LSM bucket snapshot support (CreateSnapshot/NewSnapshotBucket) and enforce snapshot immutability/safety checks.
- Switch export participant workflow to snapshot during Prepare and scan snapshots during Commit/export execution.

## Pull request overview

Adds opt-in (`MODULES_VALIDATE_BASE_URL`) validation for module `baseURL` settings and hardens outbound module HTTP clients (notably by disabling automatic redirect following) to reduce SSRF-style risks across module integrations.

**Changes:**
- Introduce a shared `ValidateBaseURLSetting` helper (and plumbing via `BaseClassSettings`) and apply it across many module class-settings validations.
- Add `modulecomponents.NewBaseHttpClient()` and migrate most module clients to use it (redirects disabled) while also standardizing JSON-unmarshal failure errors to include HTTP status.
- Add/extend unit tests across modules to cover baseURL validation behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 97 out of 97 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

title: Enhance CompareDigests functionality and optimize async replication
body:
### What's being changed:

This pull request adds a new binary protocol endpoint for efficient object digest comparison between replication nodes. It introduces a `CompareDigests` API and implements both client and server logic for sending, receiving, and interpreting binary-encoded digest records. Comprehensive unit tests are included to validate the new functionality. Additionally, minor improvements are made to the shutdown synchronization and request queue test logic.

**Replication CompareDigests API implementation:**

* Added `CompareDigests` method to `replicationClient`, which sends a binary-encoded list of object digests to a target node and decodes the binary response, enabling efficient detection of missing, stale, or deleted objects on the target. (`adapters/clients/replication.go`)
* Implemented `readCompareDigestsBinaryStream` to parse binary responses for the new protocol. (`adapters/clients/replication.go`)
* Added the `CompareDigests` method stub to `fakeReplicator` for testing. (`adapters/handlers/rest/clusterapi/fake_replicator_test.go`)

**Server-side endpoint and protocol handling:**

* Introduced the `/objects/compareDigests` POST endpoint, including request routing, URI matching, and a handler (`postCompareDigests`) that decodes the binary request, delegates to the replicator, and returns a binary response. (`adapters/handlers/rest/clusterapi/indices_replicas.go`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR78-R80) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR92-R93) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR252-R259) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR558-R645)
* Set a maximum request body size (4 MiB) for the new endpoint. (`adapters/handlers/rest/clusterapi/indices_replicas.go`)

**Testing and validation:**

* Added comprehensive unit tests for the client and server `CompareDigests` logic, including binary encoding/decoding, error cases, and flag handling. (`adapters/clients/replication_test.go`)
* Updated test imports and maintenance mode endpoint coverage to include the new endpoint. (`adapters/handlers/rest/clusterapi/indices_replicas_test.go`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-58fbff669f58982f32d3c23e449466ead5029fbcb632aacc7f032dda37e5a155R17-R20) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-58fbff669f58982f32d3c23e449466ead5029fbcb632aacc7f032dda37e5a155R29-R37) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-58fbff669f58982f32d3c23e449466ead5029fbcb632aacc7f032dda37e5a155R71-R73)

**Shutdown synchronization improvements:**

* Added `shutdownStartedCh` and a `ShuttingDown()` method to allow external synchronization on shutdown initiation, and ensured the channel is closed as soon as shutdown begins. (`adapters/handlers/rest/clusterapi/indices_replicas.go`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR60-R62) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR129) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR1147-R1149) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-edda0c88ff94e97f6c3ac75f5476b8e85ae1d1f388fc2a8282e31d3790c8232fR1200-R1206)

**Request queue test improvements:**

* Refactored work queue tests to improve synchronization and correctness when tracking accepted and rejected requests. (`adapters/handlers/rest/clusterapi/indices_replicas_test.go`)

commits.message:
Refactor async replication and hashtree management in Shard

- Introduced ForEachReplaceEntry method in Memtable to iterate over entries in a Replace-strategy memtable.
- Removed unnecessary minimalHashtreeInitialization channel and sync.Once from Shard, simplifying async replication state management.
- Updated initAsyncReplication to register flush-time hooks for hashtree consistency.
- Modified updateHashtreeOnFlush to compute deltas for hashtree updates based on flushed objects.
- Removed redundant hashtree initialization checks in batchDeleteObject, DeleteObject, and mergeObjectInStorage methods.
- Ensured that hashtree updates occur only after flushing memtables, maintaining the invariant that hashtree reflects on-disk data.
- Added tests to validate async replication lifecycle and hashtree updates during flush operations.

feat: enhance hashtree flushing logic during shard shutdown to prevent stale persistence

title: Move MCP server from separate port to /v1/mcp
body:
## Summary

Moves the MCP server from a dedicated HTTP port (default 9000) onto the main REST API at `/v1/mcp`. This eliminates a separate port, simplifies deployment (one fewer port to expose/configure), and lets MCP inherit all existing REST middleware (auth, CORS, monitoring).

## How it works

The key insight is that go-swagger's code generation can be used as a clean passthrough to `mcp-go`'s `ServeHTTP` handler:

1. **Swagger spec defines `/mcp` with no body parameters** — This means go-swagger's generated `BindRequest()` only binds path/query/header params and never reads `r.Body`. The raw JSON-RPC stream remains intact for `mcp-go` to consume.

2. **`ResponderFunc` gives raw `http.ResponseWriter` access** — Instead of returning a typed response, each handler returns a `ResponderFunc` that calls `mcpHandler.ServeHTTP(w, params.HTTPRequest)` directly. This enables SSE streaming and the full MCP Streamable HTTP transport.

3. **Three HTTP methods on one path** — POST (JSON-RPC requests), GET (SSE stream), DELETE (session termination) are all defined in the swagger spec and routed by go-swagger to the same underlying `mcp-go` handler.

The result: zero middleware intercepts needed, no custom routing, and all MCP traffic flows through the standard go-swagger framework.

## Changes

- **`adapters/handlers/mcp/server.go`** — Replace `Serve()` (separate HTTP server) with `Handler()` returning `http.Handler`
- **`adapters/handlers/rest/mcp.go`** — Deleted (launched MCP in a goroutine on separate port)
- **`openapi-specs/schema.json`** — Add `/mcp` path with POST/GET/DELETE operations (no body params)
- **`adapters/handlers/rest/configure_api.go`** — Wire generated handlers via `ResponderFunc` passthrough to `mcp-go`
- **`usecases/config/config_handler.go`** — Remove `Port` from MCP config struct
- **`usecases/config/environment.go`** — Remove `DefaultMCPPort` and `MCP_SERVER_PORT` env var parsing
- **`usecases/config/operational_mode.go`** — Add `mcp` to all operational mode whitelists (read-only, write-only, scale-out)
- **`test/docker/weaviate.go`** — MCP endpoint now derived from HTTP endpoint (`<host>:<rest-port>/v1/mcp`)
- **`docker-compose-mcp-test.yml`** — Remove `9002:9000` port mapping
- **Test URL updates** — All test files updated from `localhost:9002/mcp` to `localhost:8082/v1/mcp`

## Removed

- `MCP_SERVER_PORT` env var — no longer needed since MCP shares the main REST port
- Separate port 9000 exposure in Docker configs
- `startMcpServer` goroutine launcher

title: test: robust cluster startup, node rename, and CI test image builds
body:
## Summary

Makes testcontainer-based acceptance tests robust and diagnosable. Changes span `test/docker/`, `test/run.sh`, and test files.

### Cluster startup overhaul (`test/docker/compose.go`)
- **2-phase startup** (live → ready) with retries. All nodes start concurrently using `/v1/.well-known/live` (phase 1), then wait for `/v1/.well-known/ready` (phase 2). Matches k8s pod lifecycle and eliminates the deadlock where Raft quorum required all nodes, but nodes waited sequentially.
- **Retry on startup failure** — up to 3 retries per node (90s each), mimicking k8s pod restart. Previously a single transient memberlist failure killed the test.

### Node rename (`test/docker/weaviate.go`, `compose.go`, test files)
- Container names now match `CLUSTER_HOSTNAME` using K8s StatefulSet convention: `weaviate-0`, `weaviate-1`, `weaviate-2`.
- Eliminates the confusing `node1/node2/node3` ↔ `weaviate/weaviate2/weaviate3` mapping.
- All test files updated to use `docker.Weaviate0`/`Weaviate1`/`Weaviate2` constants.

### StopAt polling (`test/docker/docker.go`)
- Replaced fixed 3s `time.Sleep` with polling `/v1/nodes` on a surviving node (2s per-request timeout, 30s total).
- Handles quorum loss gracefully — HTTP errors/timeouts mean the node is already detected as down.

### Error annotations (`test/docker/weaviate.go`, `docker.go`, `compose.go`)
- Every operation that could hit `context.DeadlineExceeded` now includes container name, operation phase, and elapsed time.
- Container logs (last 50 lines) embedded in startup crash errors.
- No more bare "context deadline exceeded" in CI logs.

### CI test image builds (`test/run.sh`)
- Extracted `build_weaviate_test_image()` helper — builds the Docker image once and exports `TEST_WEAVIATE_IMAGE`. Idempotent (skips if already set).
- Fixed `compaction` and `compaction-recovery` jobs that were missing the image build, causing testcontainers to build from source and exceed startup timeouts.
- Deduplicated the build+export pattern across 8 functions.

### Split distributed_tasks from fast-group-4 (`.github/workflows`, `test/run.sh`)
- Gives crash recovery tests their own CI job with 45-minute timeout.

## Test plan
- [x] `TestCrashRecovery_SingleNodeRestart` passes locally (55s)
- [x] `TestCrashRecovery_MajorityRestart` passes locally (69s) — quorum loss scenario
- [x] `TestMultiNode_RollingRestartAfterComplete` 10/10 flakiness runs
- [x] CI: 51/51 green on previous push
- [ ] CI: all checks pass after build_weaviate_test_image refactor

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: etiennedi

reviews.body: ### Orca Security Scan Summary | Status | Check | Issues by priority | | | ------- | ----- | ------------------ | - | | Passed | Infrastructure as Code | 0   0   0   0 | View in Orca | | Passed | SAST | 0   0   0   0 |

commits.message:
test: robust cluster startup with live/ready separation, retry, and log capture

Four improvements to test/docker cluster startup:

1. Separate liveness from readiness, matching k8s behavior:
   Phase 1 starts all nodes concurrently with /v1/.well-known/live
   (process running, ports listening). Phase 2 waits for
   /v1/.well-known/ready (Raft quorum formed, API serving). This
   prevents deadlock: Raft bootstrap needs all nodes running, so we
   can't wait for node 1 to be "ready" before starting nodes 2/3.

2. Add retry logic for all nodes (up to 3 retries): mimics k8s pod
   restart behavior during bootstrap. Both phase 1 retries and phase 2
   readiness use independent contexts (derived from context.Background)
   so slow retries don't exhaust the parent test context and cause
   cascading deadline failures in subsequent steps.

3. Replace hardcoded 3s sleep in StopAt with polling: polls a
   surviving node's /v1/nodes endpoint until the stopped node is no
   longer HEALTHY. Adapts to actual memberlist detection speed.

4. Capture container logs on startup crash: when a container exits
   with code 1, the last 50 lines of logs are embedded in the error
   message so CI shows the actual crash reason.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename cluster nodes to weaviate-{0,1,2} and annotate context errors

- Rename container constants: Weaviate0="weaviate-0", Weaviate1="weaviate-1",
  Weaviate2="weaviate-2" (K8s StatefulSet convention). Container name now
  equals CLUSTER_HOSTNAME, eliminating the node1/node2/node3 mapping.
- Annotate all context-dependent errors in startWeaviate (PostStart hook,
  GenericContainer, PortEndpoint calls) so deadline exceeded errors identify
  exactly which operation and container timed out.
- Store env settings on weaviate DockerContainer (was nil) so StopAt can
  look up CLUSTER_HOSTNAME for polling.
- Update all test files referencing old "node1"/"node2"/"node3" hostnames.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix errorlint: use %w for error wrapping in StopAt/StartAt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extract build_weaviate_test_image helper, fix missing image builds

- Extract build_weaviate_test_image() to deduplicate the Docker image
  build + TEST_WEAVIATE_IMAGE export that was copy-pasted across 8
  functions in test/run.sh.
- Add image build to compaction and compaction-recovery jobs that were
  missing it, causing testcontainers to build from source and exceed
  the 90s startup timeout on CI.
- The helper is idempotent: skips if TEST_WEAVIATE_IMAGE is already set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Give compaction CI job 45-minute timeout

The compaction job now builds the Docker image via build_weaviate_test_image,
which needs time on top of the test runtime. 15 minutes is no longer enough.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Assign static IPs to weaviate containers to survive restarts

When a container is stopped and restarted, Docker may assign a new IP.
The Raft cluster caches the old IP and can't resolve the restarted node,
causing readiness timeouts in crash recovery tests.

Fix: create the test network with a fixed IPAM subnet (10.99.0.0/16) and
assign static IPs per weaviate node (10.99.0.10/11/12). These IPs are
preserved across stop/start cycles, so Raft peers can always reach each
other at their original addresses.

Verified locally:
- TestCrashRecovery_MajorityRestart: PASS (61s)
- TestCrashRecovery_SingleNodeRestart: PASS (55s)
- TestCrashRecovery_RollingRestart: PASS (49s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Derive static IPs from hostname, preserve IP on network reconnect

Instead of passing static IPs explicitly, derive them deterministically
from the container hostname via StaticIPForHostname(). This ensures every
weaviate-{0,1,2} container always gets the same IP regardless of how it
was created.

Also apply static IPs in ConnectToNetwork via --ip flag. Without this,
docker network connect assigns a new IP, and the Raft cluster can't
resolve the rejoining node — causing TestNetworkIsolationSplitBrain to
fail with "2 nodes instead of 3".

Verified locally:
- TestNetworkIsolationSplitBrain: PASS (node rejoined in 21s)
- TestCrashRecovery_MajorityRestart: PASS (61s)
- TestCrashRecovery_SingleNodeRestart: PASS (55s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Improve parsing of property length in BMW segments
body: ### What's being changed: e2e: https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-e2e-tests/actions/runs/23456995648 chaos: https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-chaos-engineering/actions/runs/23456999912 The gob library has A TON of allocations when encoding and (bigger problem) deconding values. We're using it to read the property lengths attributes for BMW segments. This PR replaces the gob library with a custom encoder/decoder that is fully compatible with gob for map[uint64]uint32 with fallback to the gob decdoer for other types. In addition there is a test suite that makes sure that the gob-data format is not changing when updating (it has been stable since go 1.0, but just to be sure) | | Custom (gobenc) | Gob (stdlib) | Improvement | |-----------|-----------------|--------------|-------------| | **Encode** | 20.8 µs · 1 alloc · 6 KiB | 39.0 µs · 2,027 allocs · 48 KiB | 1.9x faster · 2027x fewer allocs | | **Decode** | 10.7 µs · 7 allocs · 37 KiB | 68.0 µs · 177 allocs · 87 KiB | 6.3x faster · 25x fewer allocs | Profile from before: ### Review checklist - [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. author: amourao

reviews.body: ### Orca Security Scan Summary | Status | Check | Issues by priority | | | ------- | ----- | ------------------ | - | | Passed | Secrets | 0   0   0   0 | View in Orca |

## Pull request overview

This PR significantly reduces heap allocations during LSM compaction for the `replace` strategy. It achieves this by (1) introducing a reusable cursor (`segmentCursorReplaceReusable`) that buffers node data across iterations and reuses internal byte slices, (2) pre-computing primary and secondary index sizes during the merge loop to avoid a redundant O(N) scan, and (3) replacing the two-phase scratch-file approach for writing indexes with a single forward-pass direct write. This results in a ~95% reduction in total allocated memory and ~89% fewer GC cycles, with no change in compaction time.

**Changes:**
- New `segmentCursorReplaceReusable` cursor with reusable buffers; `compactorReplace` now uses it instead of `innerCursorReplaceAllKeys`
- New `MarshalSortedKeysFromKeys`/`marshalSortedSecondaryFromKeys`/`computePrimaryIndexSize`/`computeSecondaryIndexSize` functions in `segmentindex/tree.go` for scratch-file-free index serialization
- `Indexes.WriteTo` simplified: scratch files and in-memory paths removed and replaced with `writeDirectly`; pre-computed sizes carried via new `SizesPrecomputed`/`PrecomputedPrimaryIndexSize`/`PrecomputedSecondaryIndexSizes` fields

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `cursor_segment_replace.go` | Adds `offsetReader` and `segmentCursorReplaceReusable` for allocation-free sequential iteration |
| `compactor_replace.go` | Switches to reusable cursor, accumulates index sizes during merge loop, copies keys via keyArena |
| `segmentindex/tree.go` | New BST serialization functions that operate directly on `[]Key` without intermediate `Node`/`Tree` structures |
| `segmentindex/indexes.go` | Removes scratch-file / in-memory branching; single `writeDirectly` path using pre-computed sizes |
| `segment_serialization.go` | Merges tombstone+

title: Improve UX when mistakenly sending "2" or "2.0" when filtering on int/number props
body:
### What's being changed:

Currently, if a user mistakenly sends a filter on an integer or number property with a string value then the API layer validation is silently skipped. Instead of throwing in `traverser.GetClass`, the issue only arises in the index leading to query latency in replicated setups where the error on a follower node was being retried needlessly due to the index-level error

This change introduces more coercion in the gRPC layer to convert mistakenly sent strings into their correct types on the server to avoid the above issue while also improving the UX of such cases. Now, if a user sends a string value filter on an int/number property then the server will silently perform the required coercion and the query will complete successfully. In cases where actual strings are sent, the gRPC layer still errors out returning an error to the client

### Review checklist

title: fix: nil pointer panic in UpdateLastUsedTimestamp for unknown users
body:
## Summary

- Fixes a nil pointer dereference panic in `DBUser.UpdateLastUsedTimestamp` that crashes all nodes when the usage metrics logic runs and encounters a user ID that doesn't exist in the local user map.

## Root Cause

`UpdateLastUsedTimestamp` receives a map of user IDs and timestamps from remote nodes via the cluster API (`RemoteApiKey.GetUserStatus`). It accesses `c.data.Users[userID]` (a `map[string]*User`) without checking for nil. When a user ID exists on one node but not locally (e.g., recently deleted user, or RAFT state not yet fully synced), the map returns `nil` and `.LastUsedAt.Before(lastUsed)` panics.

This was observed in production on v1.36.6 across all three nodes in a WCD cluster, causing pod restarts and user impact. The secondary panics seen in the replication path (`putOverwriteObjects`/`Unmarshal`) were collateral from the node crash-restart turbulence.

## Fix

Added a nil guard: if the user ID is not present locally, skip the update. This is correct behavior — we should not update timestamps for users we don't know about.

## Test plan

- [x] Added `TestUpdateLastUsedTimestamp_NonExistentUser` that verifies no panic when updating timestamps for non-existent users
- [x] Existing `TestLastUsedTime` continues to pass (normal update flow unaffected)
- [x] Full `apikey` package test suite passes with `-race` (18/18 tests)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: jfrancoa

Release notes

What's Changed

  • Update README to include Node.js 24 support details and requirements by @​salmanmkc in actions/checkout#2248

Updated persist-credentials to store the credentials under $RUNNER_TEMP instead of directly in the local git config.

fix: ensure original objects are preserved during read-repair in CheckConsistency

Signed-off-by: Jeronimo <jeronimo.irazabal@gmail.com>
fix: update CheckConsistency to retain original objects during consistency checks

Add DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT env var to override default shard count

Weaviate defaults shardingConfig.desiredCount to the cluster node count.
This works well for spreading collections evenly, but some deployments
need to overshard for future flexibility (e.g., 12 shards on a 3-node
cluster). Since sharding config is immutable after creation, operators
need infrastructure-level control without requiring every collection
creation to explicitly set it.

Follow the established DEFAULT_QUANTIZATION pattern: a DynamicValue[int]
set via env var and overridable at runtime. When DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT
is > 0, it replaces the node count as the default desiredCount. User-
explicit desiredCount in the class creation request still takes
precedence, and multi-tenancy collections are unaffected.

Also adds WithWeaviateFiles to the test docker infrastructure, enabling
tests to pre-create files inside containers before startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
fix: sets FullParser as cron schedule's parser

Merge pull request #10798 from weaviate/fix_ttl_cron_full_parser

fix: sets FullParser as cron schedule's parser

Avoid halting compactions during backup (#10677)

* Avoid halting compactions during backup:
- Use hard-links to reference segment files as a DB "snapshot"
- .db files are immutable after creation so no risk of data loss
- Backup then runs on the hard-linked files allowing compactions to continue making new compacted .db files
- Increases disk utilisation while backups are running but does not pause compactions

* Appease golangci-lint

* Handle case where collection was dropped during backup, need to clear up dir

* Fallback to standard impl if hard-links aren't supported

* Fix unit tests due to bad merge conflict resolution

Address review findings: add upper bound, RAFT comment, cleanup

- Cap DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT at 512 to prevent accidental resource
  exhaustion from misconfiguration
- Add comment explaining RAFT safety of the override (runs before
  proposal, so all nodes see the same resolved value)
- Add godoc to WithWeaviateFiles test helper

commits.message:
fix: ensure original objects are preserved during read-repair in CheckConsistency

Signed-off-by: Jeronimo <jeronimo.irazabal@gmail.com>
fix: update CheckConsistency to retain original objects during consistency checks

Add DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT env var to override default shard count

Weaviate defaults shardingConfig.desiredCount to the cluster node count.
This works well for spreading collections evenly, but some deployments
need to overshard for future flexibility (e.g., 12 shards on a 3-node
cluster). Since sharding config is immutable after creation, operators
need infrastructure-level control without requiring every collection
creation to explicitly set it.

Follow the established DEFAULT_QUANTIZATION pattern: a DynamicValue[int]
set via env var and overridable at runtime. When DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT
is > 0, it replaces the node count as the default desiredCount. User-
explicit desiredCount in the class creation request still takes
precedence, and multi-tenancy collections are unaffected.

Also adds WithWeaviateFiles to the test docker infrastructure, enabling
tests to pre-create files inside containers before startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Avoid halting compactions during backup (#10677)

* Avoid halting compactions during backup:
- Use hard-links to reference segment files as a DB "snapshot"
- .db files are immutable after creation so no risk of data loss
- Backup then runs on the hard-linked files allowing compactions to continue making new compacted .db files
- Increases disk utilisation while backups are running but does not pause compactions

* Appease golangci-lint

* Appease linter

* Handle case where collection was dropped during backup, need to clear up dir

* Respond to review

* Fallback to standard impl if hard-links aren't supported

* Add acceptance test proving compactions run during a backup

* Use `t.Logf` in test instead of `fmt.Printf`

* Fix unit tests due to bad merge conflict resolution

Address review findings: add upper bound, RAFT comment, cleanup

- Cap DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT at 512 to prevent accidental resource
  exhaustion from misconfiguration
- Add comment explaining RAFT safety of the override (runs before
  proposal, so all nodes see the same resolved value)
- Add godoc to WithWeaviateFiles test helper
- Improve acceptance test cleanup with deferred class deletion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace nolint:errcheck with explicit _ = assignment

title: Add unit tracking to Distributed Task Manager
body:
## Why

The DTM currently tracks task completion at the node level: each node reports "I'm done"
and the task finishes when all nodes have reported. This breaks down for operations like
runtime reindexing where:

1. **Uneven shard distribution.** In a 3-node cluster with RF=1, shards may land on only
   2 of 3 nodes. The third node has nothing to do but must still report completion.
2. **RF > 1 requires per-replica tracking.** A shard with RF=3 has one replica per node.
   Each replica must be reindexed independently. Node-level tracking cannot distinguish
   "node A finished replica 1 of shard S" from "node A finished all its work".
3. **Completion barriers.** Some operations (e.g. changing tokenization) require ALL shards
   to finish reindexing before ANY shard swaps to the new format. Node-level tracking
   provides no barrier primitive.
4. **Per-tenant atomicity.** In multi-tenant collections, each tenant's replicas should
   swap independently as they complete, without waiting for other tenants. This requires
   grouping sub-units by tenant.

This PR adds **sub-unit tracking** and **group-level finalization** to the DTM, **replacing**
the legacy node-level tracking entirely. No runtime reindex code changes — that will build
on top of this in a follow-up.

**RFC:** [DTM Sub-Unit Tracking and Group Finalization](https://www.notion.so/31d70562ccd68198a884dfd6eb523000)

## Design

**Sub-units are always required.** Every task must declare at least one sub-unit at creation
time. Legacy node-level tracking (`FinishedNodes`, `RecordNodeCompletion`, `HasSubUnits()`)
has been removed — any "every node must run" use case is expressed as one sub-unit per node.
This is an internal breaking change with no external API impact, confirmed safe by
@antas-marcin since the legacy mode had no production callers.

**Deterministic sub-unit assignment (primary pattern).** The Provider stores a `SubUnitToNode`
map in the task payload at creation time. During `StartTask`, the Provider checks this map
and only processes sub-units assigned to the local node. This is the primary use case for
reindexing: the task creator knows which node owns which shard-replica. Deterministic
assignment avoids Raft contention entirely — no races, no wasted work. An implicit
race-based fallback exists for work-stealing patterns but is secondary.

**Group-level finalization.** Groups exist primarily for multi-tenant reindexing: each
tenant's shard-replicas form a group, so one tenant can finish and swap to the new index
while other tenants are still reindexing. Without groups, every sub-unit must complete before
any finalization can happen — a slow tenant would block all others.

If the Provider implements `SubUnitAwareProvider`, the Scheduler fires two callbacks:
- `OnGroupCompleted(task, groupID, localSubUnitIDs)` — fires per group when all sub-units
  in that group reach a terminal state. Can fire mid-flight while the task is still STARTED.
- `OnTaskCompleted(task)` — fires once per node after ALL sub-units are terminal. Use for
  global operations like Raft schema updates (deduplicated across nodes).

**Fail-fast on sub-unit failure.** When any sub-unit fails, the task immediately transitions
to FAILED. In-flight sub-units are terminated — their subsequent reports are rejected. Both
callbacks still fire with `task.Status == FAILED` so providers can roll back or finalize
partial results.

**Progress throttling.** Sub-unit progress updates go through Raft. `ThrottledRecorder`
rate-limits to one update per 30s per sub-unit. Completion/failure calls are never throttled.

**Per-node concurrency limiter.** A channel-based semaphore lets providers limit how many
sub-units are processed concurrently on a single node, preventing resource exhaustion.

## Legacy removal (internal breaking change)

The legacy node-level tracking mode has been removed per reviewer feedback from @antas-marcin.
The sub-unit mode is strictly more capable — there are no production callers of the legacy
mode, so removing it simplifies the codebase by eliminating all `HasSubUnits()` branching.

Specifically removed:
- `Task.FinishedNodes` field and `Task.HasSubUnits()` method
- `Manager.RecordNodeCompletion()` method
- `RecordDistributedTaskNodeCompletion` / `RecordDistributedTaskNodeFailure` from the
  `TaskCompletionRecorder` interface and `ThrottledRecorder`
- Legacy Raft endpoint methods
- Legacy branches in `Scheduler.filterStartedTasks()` and `ShardNoopProvider.StartTask()`
- `Store.numberOfNodesInTheCluster()` (now unused)

Kept for backward compat:
- `TYPE_DISTRIBUTED_TASK_RECORD_NODE_COMPLETED (302)` in proto — now a no-op with warning log
- `RecordDistributedTaskNodeCompletionRequest` proto message — kept to avoid breaking Raft
  log deserialization

## Crash recovery

After a node crash, the Scheduler automatically re-launches tasks with non-terminal sub-units:
1. `Scheduler.Start()` fetches all tasks from Raft (persistent state survives restarts)
2. `filterStartedTasks()` returns tasks where the local node has non-terminal sub-units
3. `provider.StartTask()` re-launches; the provider skips already-completed sub-units
4. Dead handle detection in `tick()` allows re-launch if a provider goroutine exits

Sub-units claimed by a crashed node (`IN_PROGRESS`, `NodeID == crashedNode`) are only
re-processable by that same node on restart. The framework does NOT reassign them.

**Acceptance tests** verify four crash scenarios in a 3-node cluster (RF=3, 3 shards, 9
sub-units with per-replica assignment):
- `TestCrashRecovery_SingleNodeRestart` — one non-leader node crashes mid-flight and restarts
- `TestCrashRecovery_MajorityRestart` — 2 of 3 nodes crash, surviving node finishes its
  sub-units, crashed nodes restart and complete theirs
- `TestCrashRecovery_RollingRestart` — sequential crash-restart cycles while one node stays up
- `TestCrashRecovery_CrashBeforeAnyProgress` — node crashes before reporting any progress;
  its PENDING sub-units are re-claimed on restart

## Compatibility

- New proto fields are additive (field 6 on `AddDistributedTaskRequest`)
- New Apply types 304/305: old nodes hit the default case in `store_apply.go` which logs
  an error but does NOT panic
- Deprecated Apply type 302: kept as a no-op with warning log for rolling upgrades
- Snapshot JSON uses `omitempty` — old nodes ignore the unknown `subUnits` field on restore
- Sub-unit tasks should only be created after all nodes are upgraded (operator responsibility)

## What to review carefully

- `manager.go:RecordSubUnitCompletion` — the fail-fast state machine logic
- `scheduler.go` — group + task callback dispatch and deduplication
- `throttled_recorder.go` — cleanup on terminal events (prevents memory leaks)
- `types.go:NodeHasNonTerminalSubUnits` — treats empty NodeID as "belongs to any node"
- `crash_recovery_test.go` — four crash recovery scenarios with marker file verification

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: manager state machine (add/complete/fail/progress/snapshot/validation),
  scheduler (callbacks fire exactly once for success + failure), throttled recorder
  (interval enforcement, cleanup on terminal events), concurrency limiter
- [x] Acceptance tests: sub-unit success/failure lifecycle, per-replica mode with real
  collections, group finalization (independent groups, group failure, many groups, default
  group), crash recovery (4 scenarios)
- [x] Legacy removal: all `HasSubUnits()` branching removed, `AddTask` rejects empty
  sub-units, TYPE 302 handled as no-op, all tests migrated to sub-unit based patterns
- [x] Linting: `golangci-lint run` + `./tools/linter_go_routines.sh`

```
go test -race -count 1 ./cluster/distributedtask/...
go test -count 1 -timeout 15m ./test/acceptance/distributed_tasks/...
```

commits.message:
Add sub-unit tracking to Distributed Task Manager

Extend the DTM to track task completion at the sub-unit level (e.g.,
per-shard) instead of only at the node level. This enables fine-grained
progress visibility, a completion barrier that fires only when ALL
sub-units finish, and a global OnTaskCompleted callback for post-task
actions.

Key changes:
- Protobuf: new Apply types 304/305 for sub-unit completion/progress
- Types: SubUnit struct, SubUnitAwareProvider interface, extended
  TaskCompletionRecorder with sub-unit methods
- Manager: RecordSubUnitCompletion and UpdateSubUnitProgress state
  machine methods with automatic task status transitions
- Scheduler: sub-unit-aware filterStartedTasks, OnTaskCompleted
  callback firing, ThrottledRecorder wrapping
- ThrottledRecorder: rate-limits progress updates (30s/sub-unit)
- Raft: FSM dispatch + endpoint methods for sub-unit operations
- REST API: DistributedTaskSubUnit model in swagger, handler mapping
- Acceptance tests: dummy provider + debug endpoint + 3 e2e tests
  (success, failure, legacy backward compat)

Backward compatible: nil SubUnits = legacy node-level tracking path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix review findings: memory leaks, progress validation, docs, complexity

- Clean up ThrottledRecorder.lastSent entries on sub-unit completion/failure
- Clean up Scheduler.completedCallbackFired entries when tasks are removed
- Validate progress values are in [0.0, 1.0] in UpdateSubUnitProgress
- Add Godoc to all new exported symbols (types, constructors, methods)
- Extract mapSubUnits helper from ListTasks to reduce nesting depth
- Add unit tests for progress validation and throttle entry cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite docs to focus on architecture, gotchas, and non-obvious behavior

Add package-level doc.go with big picture: three-component architecture
(Manager/Scheduler/Provider), two tracking modes (legacy vs sub-unit),
sub-unit assignment mechanics, failure semantics, progress throttling,
and a guide for adding new task types.

Replace trivial "NewFoo creates a Foo" comments with docs that explain
actual gotchas and design decisions:
- Task: fail-fast on sub-unit failure, tracking mode is immutable
- SubUnit: unassigned NodeID means any node can claim it
- RecordSubUnitCompletion: why in-flight completions are rejected
- UpdateSubUnitProgress: silent ignore due to Raft ordering
- ThrottledRecorder: why it exists, cleanup lifecycle
- DummyProvider: how it's wired and what it exercises
- Handler.ListTasks: payload redaction limitation

Remove obvious constructor/type docs that add no value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce code duplication flagged by SonarCloud

Extract shared helpers to eliminate repeated patterns:
- applyDistributedTaskCommand in Raft endpoints (5 methods → 1 helper)
- findStartedSubUnitWithLock in Manager (shared validation chain)
- startDTMCluster/addTask/awaitTaskStatus/findTask in acceptance tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce test duplication flagged by SonarCloud (8% → target ≤3%)

Extract shared test helpers to eliminate repeated command construction:
- addTaskWithSubUnits, completeSubUnit, failSubUnit, updateProgress in manager_test
- initSubUnitHarness in scheduler_test
- newTestThrottledRecorder in throttled_recorder_test

Net reduction: ~195 lines across 3 test files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-sub-unit finalization (OnSubUnitsCompleted) to DTM

Extend SubUnitAwareProvider with a two-phase callback: OnSubUnitsCompleted
fires first on each node with its local sub-unit IDs (for per-shard
finalization like bucket swaps), then OnTaskCompleted fires for global
operations (like Raft schema updates). Both fire on FINISHED and FAILED
tasks. Add debug status endpoint, marker file verification, and 3-node
acceptance tests covering varied sub-unit distributions and failure paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename DummyProvider → ShardNoopProvider with collection-aware sub-unit ownership

Rename all symbols and files from DummyProvider to ShardNoopProvider, add a
ShardLister interface so the provider can determine sub-unit ownership based
on real shard topology (when a Collection is specified in the payload), and
add a dbShardLister adapter in configure_api.go. Env var renamed to
SHARD_NOOP_PROVIDER_ENABLED.

Includes 11 unit tests covering synthetic mode, collection-aware mode,
error handling, fallback behavior, callbacks, and task lifecycle.

Real-collection acceptance tests are deferred: they uncovered a scheduler
orchestration issue where task version changes cause processSubUnits restarts,
preventing other nodes from completing their shards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix scheduler dead-handle bug and add real-collection acceptance tests

The scheduler's Start() method fatally returned an error when the initial
ListDistributedTasks call failed (e.g. Raft not ready on non-leader nodes),
preventing the tick loop from ever starting. This meant shards on those nodes
were never processed. Now Start() logs the failure and starts the loop anyway,
letting tick() pick up tasks once the cluster is ready.

Additionally:
- Add Done() to TaskHandle interface for dead-handle detection in tick()
- Skip already-terminal sub-units on re-launch to avoid error spam
- Add retry logic to ShardNoopProvider recorder calls and shard listing
- Add unit test for scheduler resilience to initial listing failure
- Add 4 dead-handle unit tests (detection, no spurious restart, recovery, multi-node)
- Add 3 real-collection acceptance tests (RF1, RF2, RF1 with failure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-replica sub-unit model and comprehensive DTM acceptance tests

Replace the broken "suID = shard name" model with per-replica sub-units:
each replica of each shard gets its own sub-unit ID ({shard}__{node}).
SubUnitToShard maps sub-unit IDs to shard names for locality checks,
SubUnitToNode provides deterministic ownership to avoid RAFT contention.

Provider changes:
- Add SubUnitToShard, SubUnitToNode, SlowSubUnitID, ProcessingDelayMs
- Write processing markers (/tmp/dtm-process) and completion markers
  (/tmp/dtm-complete) for 3-phase verification in tests
- Switch debug handler from query params to JSON body

New test coverage (22 tests total, was 10):
- RF3 with 1 and 3 shards, RF2 with 24 shards (48 sub-units)
- Idle node verification (OnTaskCompleted on all nodes)
- Partial shard targeting, temporal ordering (5s barrier)
- Multi-tenant: RF1/RF3 with 10/100 tenants, partial targeting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce acceptance test duplication with table-driven helpers

Extract runStandardCollectionTest and runStandardMTTest helpers,
collapse 4 finalization tests into one table-driven test, and fix
scheduler step numbering typo. Removes ~230 lines of duplication
without changing test behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-node concurrency limiter and group-level finalization to DTM

Part 1: ConcurrencyLimiter — channel-based semaphore for provider-level
throttling of concurrent sub-unit processing. ShardNoopProvider gains
MaxConcurrency payload field for fan-out with limiter.

Part 2: Group-level finalization — SubUnit gains GroupID field (persisted
in Raft state). SubUnitAwareProvider interface changes from
OnSubUnitsCompleted to OnGroupCompleted (fires per-group mid-flight as
each group's sub-units complete) + OnTaskCompleted (fires once when task
is terminal). New proto SubUnitSpec message and AddDistributedTaskWithGroups
Raft endpoint. Default group "" preserves backward compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stale references in distributedtask doc.go

Update journey count from three to four, and rename OnSubUnitsCompleted
references to OnGroupCompleted in Journeys 2 and 3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Deduplicate group tests and sequential sub-unit processing

Consolidate 4 independent TestGroupFinalization_* tests into a single
TestGroupFinalizationSuite sharing one 3-node cluster. Refactor
sequential processSubUnits to delegate to shouldProcessSubUnit and
processOneSubUnit, eliminating ~70 lines of duplicated logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add crash recovery acceptance tests and provider idempotency docs

Four new acceptance tests verify that DTM tasks complete despite node crashes:
SingleNodeRestart, MajorityRestart (2/3 nodes), RollingRestart, and
CrashBeforeAnyProgress. Also documents the provider idempotency contract
in doc.go, clarifying crash recovery expectations for provider implementors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stale godoc reference and minor provider cleanup

Remove reference to nonexistent OnSubUnitsCompleted in
SubUnitAwareProvider doc. Fix misleading "(concurrent)" log message
in processOneSubUnit (called from both paths). Pass processingDelay
to processSubUnitsConcurrent instead of recomputing it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Final cleanup: stale godoc, log message, and crash test dedup

- Fix stale reference to nonexistent OnSubUnitsCompleted in
  SubUnitAwareProvider doc
- Remove misleading "(concurrent)" from processOneSubUnit log (called
  from both sequential and concurrent paths)
- Pass processingDelay to processSubUnitsConcurrent instead of
  recomputing it
- Extract setupCrashRecoveryTask helper to deduplicate crash recovery
  test setup (~36 lines saved)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce cognitive complexity and extract string constants for SonarCloud

- Extract bootstrapProviders/cleanupStaleTasks/startActiveTasks from
  Scheduler.Start (complexity 23 → ~8)
- Extract buildGroupSubUnits/spotCheckGroupFinalization from
  TestGroupFinalizationSuite (complexity 16 → ~12)
- Extract errTaskNotRunning helper for duplicated format string in manager.go
- Extract shardNoopNamespace constant for acceptance test helpers
- Extract testSubUnitLocal/testSubUnitRemote constants in scheduler_test.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce test duplication flagged by SonarCloud (9.1% → target ≤3%)

- shard_noop_provider_test.go: extract providerFixture with newTask,
  newTaskWithPayload, startAndAwaitCompleted helpers (-115 lines)
- scheduler_test.go: extract startSubUnitTest/startGroupTest and
  addAndLaunchSubUnitTask/addAndLaunchGroupTask helpers (-37 lines)
- crash_recovery_test.go: extract startCrashRecoveryTest and
  verifyCrashRecoveryResult helpers (-30 lines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make crash recovery tests more robust: increase timeout, remove unnecessary sleeps

- Increase context timeout from 5m to 7m to account for Raft quorum recovery
  after majority node restart (StopAt sleeps 3s each for memberlist, readiness
  check can take 30-60s per node after quorum loss)
- Remove unnecessary time.Sleep calls between stop and restart: with per-node
  sub-unit assignment, surviving nodes don't process the crashed node's sub-units,
  and verifyCrashRecoveryResult already waits up to 120s for task completion
- Remove 8s sleep in MajorityRestart: with quorum lost, the surviving node can't
  make Raft progress anyway — restarting immediately is correct

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove legacy node-level tracking from DTM

Sub-unit tracking is strictly more capable than the legacy node-level mode,
which has no production usage. This removes all HasSubUnits() branching,
FinishedNodes, RecordNodeCompletion, and makes sub-units always required
when creating a task. The deprecated Raft log type (302) is kept as a
no-op for backward compatibility during rolling upgrades.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add godoc comments to exported Manager and Scheduler methods

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce test duplication flagged by SonarCloud (3.1% → target ≤3%)

Extract shared helpers to eliminate duplicated test patterns:
- shard_noop_provider_test: use newTaskWithPayload for PerReplica tests,
  add startTaskAndAssertNoProgress helper
- scheduler_test: extract launchAndFailSubUnit helper for failure tests
- sub_unit_test/group_test: extract awaitMarkers helper shared by
  awaitProcessingMarkers, awaitFinalizedSubUnits, awaitGroupFinalizedSubUnits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TestLegacyTask_NoSubUnits: verify rejection of zero-sub-unit tasks

The "Remove legacy node-level tracking" commit now requires tasks to have
at least one sub-unit. Update the acceptance test to verify the server
correctly rejects zero-sub-unit tasks with an appropriate error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix protolint: shorten line exceeding 120-char limit in message.proto

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove accidental PR #10698 changes (periodic memberlist rejoin)

These changes belong to PR #10698 (fix flaky
TestNetworkIsolationSplitBrain) and were accidentally included on this
branch. Reverts delegate.go and state.go to main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Write noop provider markers into shard/data dirs instead of /tmp

Move marker file writes from /tmp to the Weaviate data directory to avoid
leaking side effects outside the process when running locally. Collection-aware
tasks write hidden dot-files directly into shard directories; synthetic tasks
use {dataRoot}/.dtm/ as a fallback base.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extract lock-holding code into named methods with defer unlock

Addresses reviewer feedback to ensure all mutex acquisitions use defer for
unlock. Extracted three lock-without-defer patterns into named methods:
addTask, recordFinalizedGroup, and setCompletionRecorders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace panic with clamp-to-1 in ConcurrencyLimiter

Invalid maxConcurrency values (< 1) are now silently clamped to 1 instead
of panicking, making the limiter safe against misconfiguration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename SubUnit to Unit, use Enabled() for env var, drop dead case

Three reviewer feedback items:
1. Rename SubUnit → Unit throughout internal types, proto definitions,
   and all references. JSON/wire tags preserved for API compatibility.
2. Use entconfig.Enabled() instead of string comparison for
   SHARD_NOOP_PROVIDER_ENABLED env var.
3. Remove the dead TYPE_DISTRIBUTED_TASK_RECORD_NODE_COMPLETED case
   from store_apply.go since the feature never shipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix JSON tag mismatch in test addTaskRequest after SubUnit rename

The rename agent changed JSON tags for UnitToShard, UnitToNode, and
SlowSubUnit in the test client but not in the server handler, causing
fields to be silently dropped and all collection-aware tests to hang.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Complete SubUnit→Unit rename: fix JSON tags, legacy tests, flaky timeout

- Rename all remaining SubUnit JSON tags in payload, handler, and test
  structs (failSubUnitId→failUnitId, subUnitToShard→unitToShard, etc.)
- Regenerate mocks with make mocks
- Migrate scheduler tests from removed node-level completion API to
  unit-level completion (completeUnit/failUnit helpers)
- Add Done() method to testTask to satisfy TaskHandle interface
- Increase recvWithTimeout to 5s to prevent flakiness under CPU load
  (verified with 100 consecutive runs)
- Fix doc.go examples (su-X → u-X, RecordSubUnitCompletion → RecordUnitCompletion)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Complete SubUnit→Unit rename: swagger, models, tests, acceptance

- openapi-specs/schema.json: DistributedTaskSubUnit→DistributedTaskUnit,
  subUnits→units field, update all descriptions
- Regenerate embedded_spec.go and entities/models/ from updated schema
  (distributed_task_sub_unit.go deleted, distributed_task_unit.go added)
- types.go: JSON tag subUnits→units on Task.Units field
- shard_noop_provider.go: log field subUnitCount→unitCount
- raft_distributed_tasks_apply_endpoints.go: param subUnitSpecs→unitSpecs
- Rename test functions: *SubUnit*→*Unit* in manager_test, provider_test,
  throttled_recorder_test
- acceptance tests: task.SubUnits→task.Units, DistributedTaskSubUnit→
  DistributedTaskUnit, test case names, FinalizedSubUnits→FinalizedUnits,
  rename sub_unit_test.go→unit_tracking_test.go
- usecases/distributedtask: SubUnits→Units, DistributedTaskSubUnit→Unit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix gofumpt alignment in debugStatus struct

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: chore: include switching to binary encoding for digest responses
body:
### What's being changed:

This pull request introduces several improvements to the `replicationClient` in `adapters/clients/replication.go`, focusing on more efficient binary streaming for digest operations, improved request compression, and some test refactoring for consistency. The most important changes are grouped below.

### Binary streaming and protocol enhancements

* Added support for binary streaming in `DigestObjectsInRange` and `HashTreeLevel` methods, allowing the client to efficiently read fixed-size binary records from the response, with legacy JSON fallback for older nodes. This includes new helper functions `readDigestsInRangeBinaryStream` and `readDigestsBinaryStream` for decoding these streams. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9c6b07b40ed868288b28472fd7e8e41e994cfdaad151ca02a8442e772453a922R104-R247) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9c6b07b40ed868288b28472fd7e8e41e994cfdaad151ca02a8442e772453a922R524-R550)
* Modified request headers to indicate support for and request binary-encoded responses, and updated response handling to check for binary encoding. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9c6b07b40ed868288b28472fd7e8e41e994cfdaad151ca02a8442e772453a922R104-R247) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9c6b07b40ed868288b28472fd7e8e41e994cfdaad151ca02a8442e772453a922L85-R94)

### Compression improvements

* Switched to using `zstd.NewWriter(nil)` and `EncodeAll` for request compression in `HashTreeLevel` and `OverwriteObjects`, simplifying the compression logic and ensuring consistent use of zstd compression for requests.
* Added request headers to indicate zstd compression and binary encoding for relevant methods.

### Code and type safety improvements

* Refactored `replicationClient` to be a struct embedding `retryClient` instead of a type alias, improving clarity and extensibility.
* Fixed method signatures to return only the result and error, removing named return values for better readability and consistency.

### Test improvements

* Updated all test cases to use a new `newReplicationClient(t, ...)` helper for consistent client instantiation, improving test reliability and setup. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L127-R133) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L172-R178) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L206-R212) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L252-R258) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L288-R294) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L323-R329) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L361-R370) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L399-R405) [[9]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L449-R455) [[10]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L496-R502) [[11]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L531-R537) [[12]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67L581-R587)

### Dependency and import updates

* Added new imports for `encoding/binary`, `math`, `github.com/google/uuid`, and others to support binary streaming and compression features. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9c6b07b40ed868288b28472fd7e8e41e994cfdaad151ca02a8442e772453a922R18-R30) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-4679ca5fef1e58a34c731d699ba35e69afe99b24a17f6ece435737dd85840f67R16-R37)

These changes collectively improve the efficiency, reliability, and maintainability of the replication client and its tests.

### Review checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:
- [ ] All new code is covered by tests where it is reasonable.
- [ ] Performance tests have been run or not necessary.

<!-- Uncomment the following section if this PR requires changes in related projects (e.g., documentation, client libraries).

GitHub actions will automatically create an issue in the corresponding repository for each checked box below. (See `.github/workflows/create-cross-functional-issues.yml`)

### Cross-functional impact

- [ ] This change requires public documentation (weaviate-io) to be updated. Check the box to automatically create a corresponding issue.
- Does it require a change in the client libraries? If yes, please check the boxes for the affected client libraries.
    - [ ] Python (weaviate-python-client)
    - [ ] JavaScript/TypeScript (typescript-client)
    - [ ] Go (weaviate-go-client)
    - [ ] Java (java-client)

author: jeroiraz

reviews.body: ### Orca Security Scan Summary | Status | Check | Issues by priority | | | ------- | ----- | ------------------ | - | | Passed | Secrets | 0   0   0   0 | View in Orca |

## Pull request overview This PR tweaks async replication’s hashtree parameters and optimizes the replica hashtree-level exchange by adding a binary response format and updating the replication client to request/decode it. **Changes:** - Reduce async replication hashtree height defaults/limits (single-tenant default, and maximum allowed height). - Add an optional binary encoding for `hashtree/level` responses in the replicated-indices cluster API handler. - Refactor the replication client to request binary responses for `HashTreeLevel` and add binary digest unmarshalling. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `adapters/repos/db/shard_async_replication.go` | Adjusts default and max hashtree height constants used by async replication config validation. | | `adapters/handlers/rest/clusterapi/indices_replicas.go` | Adds binary response support for hashtree level digest responses (header-gated, JSON fallback). | | `adapters/clients/replication.go` | Refactors replication client structure and adds binary digest decoding + request header negotiation for hashtree level calls. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: fix: ensure original objects are preserved during read-repair in CheckConsistency
body:
### What's being changed:

This pull request refines the read-repair logic in the `finderStream` and adds a targeted test to ensure correct behavior. The main improvement is that after a read-repair operation, the original object in the result set is no longer replaced by the newer version fetched from replicas. Instead, only its consistency flag is updated, preserving any filter criteria used by the caller. Additionally, a new test verifies this behavior.

**Read-repair logic refinement:**

* In `finder_stream.go`, updated the read-repair code so that after repairing stale objects, the original object in `batch.Data` is not replaced with the newer version; only its `IsConsistent` flag is set to true, ensuring the original object (and its properties) are preserved for the caller.

**Testing improvements:**

* Added `TestFinderCheckConsistencyRepairPreservesOriginalObject` in `finder_test.go` to verify that after read-repair, the original object pointer and its content remain unchanged except for the consistency flag, ensuring callers always receive the objects they originally retrieved.

title: Add DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT env var to override default shard count
body:
## Summary
- Adds `DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT` env var (and runtime override) that controls the default `desiredCount` for new collections, instead of using the cluster node count
- Follows the established `DEFAULT_QUANTIZATION` pattern: `DynamicValue[int]` set via env var, overridable at runtime via the runtime overrides YAML
- User-explicit `desiredCount` in class creation still takes precedence; multi-tenancy collections are unaffected
- Adds `WithWeaviateFiles` to the test docker infrastructure, enabling tests to pre-create files inside containers before startup

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests: 5 parser tests covering zero/override/user-override/nil/multi-tenancy scenarios
- [x] Acceptance tests with testcontainers:
  - No env var set → uses cluster size (3)
  - Env var set to 12 → desiredCount=12
  - Env var + user override → user wins (desiredCount=5)
  - Multi-tenancy → unaffected (desiredCount=0)
  - Env var set to 0 → uses cluster size (disabled)
  - Runtime override: start with 12, write `default_sharding_count: 8` to override file → desiredCount=8
- [x] Linters: `golangci-lint` and `linter_go_routines.sh` pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: etiennedi

reviews.body: ### Orca Security Scan Summary | Status | Check | Issues by priority | | | ------- | ----- | ------------------ | - | | Passed | Infrastructure as Code | 0   0   0   0 | View in Orca | | Passed | SAST | 0   0   0   0 |
### Orca Security Scan Summary | Status | Check | Issues by priority | | | ------- | ----- | ------------------ | - | | Passed | Secrets | 0   0   0   0 | View in Orca |

commits.message:
Add DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT env var to override default shard count

Weaviate defaults shardingConfig.desiredCount to the cluster node count.
This works well for spreading collections evenly, but some deployments
need to overshard for future flexibility (e.g., 12 shards on a 3-node
cluster). Since sharding config is immutable after creation, operators
need infrastructure-level control without requiring every collection
creation to explicitly set it.

Follow the established DEFAULT_QUANTIZATION pattern: a DynamicValue[int]
set via env var and overridable at runtime. When DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT
is > 0, it replaces the node count as the default desiredCount. User-
explicit desiredCount in the class creation request still takes
precedence, and multi-tenancy collections are unaffected.

Also adds WithWeaviateFiles to the test docker infrastructure, enabling
tests to pre-create files inside containers before startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address review findings: add upper bound, RAFT comment, cleanup

- Cap DEFAULT_SHARDING_COUNT at 512 to prevent accidental resource
  exhaustion from misconfiguration
- Add comment explaining RAFT safety of the override (runs before
  proposal, so all nodes see the same resolved value)
- Add godoc to WithWeaviateFiles test helper
- Improve acceptance test cleanup with deferred class deletion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace nolint:errcheck with explicit _ = assignment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Avoid halting compactions during backup
body:
### What's being changed:

- Use hard-links to reference segment files as a DB "snapshot"
- .db files are immutable after creation so no risk of data loss
- Backup then runs on the hard-linked files allowing compactions to continue making new compacted .db files
- Increases disk utilisation while backups are running but does not pause compactions
- Hard-linked files are cleaned once the backup is over leaving the DB in a compacted state with no leftover disk utilisation

## Pull request overview This PR cleans up export status enums by removing unused/inapplicable states at the node and shard levels. At the node level (`ExportStatusResponse`), the `SKIPPED` status is removed since skipping applies to individual shards, not the overall export. At the shard level (`ShardProgress`), `STARTED` and `CANCELED` are removed since shards don't independently start or get canceled—those states belong to the node-level export. **Changes:** - Removed `SKIPPED` from the `ExportStatusResponse` status enum (node-level export status) - Removed `STARTED` and `CANCELED` from the `ShardProgress` status enum (shard-level export status) - Updated OpenAPI spec, generated Go models, and embedded spec to reflect the enum changes ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | openapi-specs/schema.json | Removed `SKIPPED` from node-level and `STARTED`/`CANCELED` from shard-level export status enums | | entities/models/export_status_response.go | Removed `SKIPPED` enum value and constant from generated Go model | | entities/models/shard_progress.go | Removed `STARTED` and `CANCELED` enum values and constants from generated Go model | | adapters/handlers/rest/embedded_spec.go | Updated both copies of the embedded spec to match schema changes | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Achieve true BC and server-side default behaviour
body:
### What's being changed:

- Without a bool to differentiate between unsetting of `alpha` and `alpha_param`, `alpha: 0` can be accidentally used when the default is expected
- This PR adds `UseAlphaParam` to the `Hybrid` proto message so that clients can set this explicitly thereby allowing the server to know that the `alpha=0` is a true `UNSET` and that the server-side default should be used

## Pull request overview

Improves the export subsystem’s correctness and observability by tightening error classification (so REST handlers can return the right HTTP codes) and expanding end-to-end test coverage for export behaviors across backends.

**Changes:**
- Wrap backend initialization failures with `ErrExportValidation` in `Scheduler` so REST export endpoints can consistently return 422 for validation-class errors.
- Propagate “already active” export slot contention across cluster prepare calls via `ErrExportAlreadyActive` and updated HTTP status handling.
- Add substantial new integration tests for S3 exports (metadata, vectors, timestamps, validation, exclude/custom config, concurrency/cancel flows) and consolidate filesystem export tests into a single suite.

* Fix flaky TestNetworkIsolationSplitBrain with periodic memberlist rejoin

Add a periodicRejoin goroutine that attempts list.Join() every 5s when
the memberlist member count drops below RaftBootstrapExpect. This fixes
the root cause: after a network partition lasting longer than
GossipToTheDeadTime (30s), memberlist purges all dead node entries and
has no mechanism to rediscover them since Join() only runs at startup.

## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the export flow to consistently use the participant-based two-phase commit (prepare/commit/abort) approach and per-node status files, removing the legacy single-node “write terminal metadata directly” path and related shard-listing helpers.

**Changes:**
- Scheduler now always coordinates exports via 2PC across participant nodes (including single-node deployments), and Status() assembles from metadata + per-node node status files.
- Removes `ExportShardNames` from the Selector interface and DB implementation, relying on `ShardOwnership` instead.
- Updates/renames export shutdown and race tests to use participant Prepare/Commit and validate node-status writes.

title: Collection Export: Metadata fixes
body:
### What's being changed:

This PR contains three metadata related fixes. Each one is its own commit:
- Simple comment change to clarify that we do not allow to fix/redo an export. A new export must be started if the previous failed
- Add status promotion on node completion. When a node is done it checks if it is the last one and if yes, promotes the overall status. There is the possibility of a race when two nodes finish at the same time (when a node check if it is the last one, the other node is still running and vice-versa). Then the next Status call will promote the status.
- Remove the ExportPlan. The ExportPlan and the combined metadata contain a lot of the same info and over time the ExportPlan was duplicated. Remove it and use the combined metadata for its previous uses.

### Review checklist
