## Pull request overview

This PR consolidates the separate `ExportPlan` type and `export_plan.json` file into the existing `ExportMetadata` type and `export_metadata.json` file, making `ExportMetadata` the single source of truth for an export's configuration and status. It also adds liveness re-read logic to resolve races between status file writes and `IsRunning` checks, and introduces a `tryPromoteMetadata` mechanism so the last participant node can finalize the metadata without requiring a `Status()` API call.

**Changes:**
- Merged `ExportPlan` fields (`NodeAssignments`, `StartedAt`) into `ExportMetadata` and removed the separate `export_plan.json` file and associated code (`writeExportPlan`, `getExportPlan`).
- Moved `Version` field from `ExportMetadata` to `NodeStatus` and added a re-read mechanism in `assembleStatusFromMetadata` to resolve race conditions between liveness checks and status file writes.
- Added `tryPromoteMetadata` in `Participant` to eagerly write terminal metadata when the last node finishes, plus extracted `assembleNodeStatuses` as a pure function with comprehensive tests.

title: Export collection: Fix race in detecting terminal status
body:
### What's being changed:

There is a short race window when trying to get the status of nodes:
- node checks if status is terminal (success/fail/cancel) in node status files
- if not, it will check if the node is still running an export to detect if the export stopped

If the other node is uploading its terminal status between those two checks, the first will return "not finished" and the second will return "not running anymore", which is interpreted as "export died on that node and the full export failed".

To fix this, we will repeat the check on the node status file once. When the node returns that no export is running anymore, we know that the file upload has completed so the second check will get the terminal state

Since Authorize requires permission on the requested resource, this will deny principals that have backups/export permissions scoped to specific collections (e.g., backups/collections/Foo) and incorrectly return 403 for backend/init/plan errors. Consider removing this wildcard auth gate or replacing it with a best-effort check that passes for any collection-scoped backups permission (e.g., derive candidate collections and use FilterAuthorizedResources).

		}

## Pull request overview

This PR tightens authorization semantics around collection export by applying RBAC filtering at export creation time and adds end-to-end RBAC acceptance coverage to validate create/status/cancel behavior across different permission sets.

**Changes:**
- Update export scheduler to resolve classes first, then filter exportable classes via RBAC before starting an export.
- Change class selection behavior to silently ignore non-existent classes in include/exclude to avoid leaking schema existence pre-authorization.
- Add RBAC acceptance tests for export create/status/cancel and class filtering behavior; extend export test helpers to support authenticated requests.

title: optimization: object digest with partial unmarshalling
body:
### What's being changed:

This pull request introduces several changes to the asynchronous replication and object digest APIs in the database adapter layer. The main focus is on improving and refactoring the way object digests are handled for replication and repair, including interface changes, mock updates, and adjustments to default configuration parameters.

**API and Interface Refactoring:**

* Replaced the `ObjectByIDErrDeleted` method in the `ShardLike` interface with `ObjectDigestErrDeleted`, which now returns a `types.RepairResponse` instead of a full object. Added a new `ObjectDigests` method for batch retrieval of object digests. These changes are reflected in both the interface and its mock implementations. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f5ceda6e94dc8658eea9558fa308aa70db8835f5a0d07762a7cc8ea445777034L87-R87) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f5ceda6e94dc8658eea9558fa308aa70db8835f5a0d07762a7cc8ea445777034R97) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-496907f60760c7795d469b0e468fb5f4d5447889dc7494403a113a147c516597R1909-R2024) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-da3785a57e3e2760e83c3f147c52db256b03105e52549b18f84f1a98c9a31e28L247-R251) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-da3785a57e3e2760e83c3f147c52db256b03105e52549b18f84f1a98c9a31e28R352-L358)

* Updated all relevant code paths to use the new digest API, including replacing usages of `ObjectByIDErrDeleted` with `ObjectDigestErrDeleted` and `MultiObjectByID` with `ObjectDigests` where appropriate. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-43faf3509d2b0cde090504767f99a960363ab7a7d4075b7ab8c3f56b0da1331aL616-R618) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-43faf3509d2b0cde090504767f99a960363ab7a7d4075b7ab8c3f56b0da1331aL749-R756) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-43faf3509d2b0cde090504767f99a960363ab7a7d4075b7ab8c3f56b0da1331aL766-R776)

**Mock and Test Code Updates:**

* Refactored the `MockShardLike` mock implementation to support the new digest methods, including removal of the old `ObjectByIDErrDeleted` mock and addition of mocks for `ObjectDigestErrDeleted` and `ObjectDigests`. Adjusted related test helpers and return signatures. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-496907f60760c7795d469b0e468fb5f4d5447889dc7494403a113a147c516597L1785-L1845) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-496907f60760c7795d469b0e468fb5f4d5447889dc7494403a113a147c516597R1909-R2024)

* Updated the mock for `ListBackupFiles` to return a slice of strings and an error, matching its real signature. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-496907f60760c7795d469b0e468fb5f4d5447889dc7494403a113a147c516597L1448-R1474) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-496907f60760c7795d469b0e468fb5f4d5447889dc7494403a113a147c516597L1484-R1501)

**Replication and Compaction Logic:**

* Modified the compaction and hashtree initialization logic to use the new digest callback signature, passing UUID bytes and update time instead of full objects, and updated related logic to match. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9658bb68e1a9d3f3d4ae83104656f4dc7c6cf22d931592c53971acdda7225e28L400-R400) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9658bb68e1a9d3f3d4ae83104656f4dc7c6cf22d931592c53971acdda7225e28L440-R448) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-9658bb68e1a9d3f3d4ae83104656f4dc7c6cf22d931592c53971acdda7225e28L463-R473) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-9d2de57ecbf268ba95c556c7d63fa41defee384d9c3e6b118e991492717de210L260-R263) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-9d2de57ecbf268ba95c556c7d63fa41defee384d9c3e6b118e991492717de210L272-R280)

**Configuration and Defaults:**

* Adjusted default and maximum values for async replication workers and hashtree height, and introduced new constants for min/max validation. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9d2de57ecbf268ba95c556c7d63fa41defee384d9c3e6b118e991492717de210L47-R49) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9d2de57ecbf268ba95c556c7d63fa41defee384d9c3e6b118e991492717de210R64-R68) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-4f91fe61f6a7baa52e723c4fdce2ef55b193172cd9c4f3171c80f8cdd87183beL44-R44)

**Cleanup:**

* Removed an unnecessary import of the `math` package from `index_async_replication.go`.

These changes collectively improve the efficiency, clarity, and maintainability of the replication and repair logic, especially in how object digests are queried and processed.

title: Fix flaky TestNetworkIsolationSplitBrain with periodic memberlist rejoin
body:
## Problem

`TestNetworkIsolationSplitBrain` is flaky across all branches. The test creates a 3-node cluster, disconnects node 3 from the Docker network, verifies only 2 nodes remain, reconnects node 3, then expects all 3 nodes to be healthy again. It frequently times out — still seeing only 2 nodes after the full 360s polling window.

## Root Cause

**Memberlist loses all knowledge of disconnected nodes and has no rejoin mechanism.**

The chain of events:

1. Node 3 is disconnected via `docker network disconnect`
2. With `MEMBERLIST_FAST_FAILURE_DETECTION=true`: `SuspicionMult=1`, `DeadNodeReclaimTime=1s`
3. Nodes 1/2 mark node 3 as dead within ~3 seconds, reclaim its name slot after 1 more second
4. Node 3 similarly marks nodes 1/2 as dead
5. After `GossipToTheDeadTime` (30s, from `DefaultLANConfig`), dead entries are purged from memberlist's internal node list via `resetNodes()`
6. The test waits up to 60s for the 2-node state, then reconnects node 3

After reconnect, **nobody knows about anybody**:

| Mechanism | Only targets alive nodes? | Can rediscover? |
|-----------|--------------------------|-----------------|
| `pushPull()` | Yes — filters `State != StateAlive` | No |
| `probe()` | Yes — skips `DeadOrLeft()` | No |
| `gossip()` | Only within `GossipToTheDeadTime` | No (expired) |
| `Join()` | One-time at startup | No re-attempt |

Node 3's memberlist has an empty alive list, so `pushPull()` finds no peers. Nodes 1/2 have no record of node 3, so they never probe or gossip to it. `Join()` was only called once during initialization. **Result: node 3 is permanently orphaned.**

The test was flaky rather than always-failing because timing determines whether dead-node entries survive long enough for gossip to re-establish contact before purge.

## Fix

Add a `periodicRejoin` goroutine to the cluster state that runs every 5 seconds. When the memberlist member count drops below `RaftBootstrapExpect`, it calls `list.Join()` with the original join addresses to re-establish contact with the cluster.

This is safe because:
- **`list.Join()` is idempotent** — calling it when already connected is a no-op (memberlist deduplicates by node name)
- **It only activates when degraded** — the goroutine skips the Join call entirely when `NumMembers() >= expectedNodes`
- **It uses existing join addresses** — the same addresses used at startup, no new configuration needed
- **It runs at low frequency** — 5-second interval, single `Join()` call, negligible overhead
- **It mirrors what operators do manually** — when a node gets orphaned today, the fix is to restart it so `Join()` runs again at init. This automates that recovery.

Note: this is not a test-only fix. The same scenario can happen in production during network partitions that last longer than `GossipToTheDeadTime`. The periodic rejoin makes the cluster self-healing.

### Secondary changes

- **Memberlist event logging**: `NotifyJoin`, `NotifyLeave`, `NotifyUpdate` now log node name and address. Previously these were no-ops (except `NotifyLeave` which cleaned the cache), making cluster membership changes invisible in logs.
- **Wire `RaftBootstrapExpect`**: The field existed in the cluster config struct but was never populated from the raft config. Now set before `cluster.Init()`.
- **Test diagnostics**: Added polling logs to the test so future failures show which nodes are seen and their status.

## Validation

- **20/20 local runs**: zero failures
- **20/20 CI runs** ([proof](https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/actions/runs/22826545051)): ran the recovery test 20 times in parallel on CI — all passed (6-8 min each)
- All linters pass (`golangci-lint`, goroutine linter)

commits.message:
Fix flaky TestNetworkIsolationSplitBrain with periodic memberlist rejoin

Add a periodicRejoin goroutine that attempts list.Join() every 5s when
the memberlist member count drops below RaftBootstrapExpect. This fixes
the root cause: after a network partition lasting longer than
GossipToTheDeadTime (30s), memberlist purges all dead node entries and
has no mechanism to rediscover them since Join() only runs at startup.

CI: run recovery test 20x in parallel to verify flakiness is fixed.

title: !feat(shards): dynamic lazy load shards
body:
### What's being changed:
This PR introduces dynamic (auto-detected) lazy shard loading, deciding per-collection whether shards should be lazy-loaded based on multi-tenancy plus shard-count and/or total shard-size thresholds. It also adds config/environment plumbing for the thresholds and supporting utilities/tests.

Changes:

- Add schema reader support for `LocalShardsCount` and use it during DB startup to decide per-index lazy loading via applyLazyShardAutoDetection.
- Add env-configurable thresholds (`LAZY_LOAD_SHARD_COUNT_THRESHOLD`, `LAZY_LOAD_SHARD_SIZE_THRESHOLD_GB`) and corresponding config fields/defaults.
- Add directory-size computation utility and tests for auto-detection + shard-size calculation behavior.


Breaking changes
- add log lines mentioning deprecation of environment variables, given that they will be deprecated  in next release version 
   - `DISABLE_LAZY_LOAD_SHARDS` 
   - `HNSW_STARTUP_WAIT_FOR_VECTOR_CACHE`  
   
 - Behaviour change 
    -  all shards per collection will be loaded until reached the thresholds 
       - tenants count in collection crossed `default: 1000`, configured via `LAZY_LOAD_SHARD_COUNT_THRESHOLD` 
       - tenant size crossed `default: 100 GB` , configured via `LAZY_LOAD_SHARD_SIZE_THRESHOLD_GB`
       - if `LAZY_LOAD_SHARD_COUNT_THRESHOLD=0` all shards will be lazy loaded 
    - `HNSW_STARTUP_WAIT_FOR_VECTOR_CACHE=true` by default
     
### Review checklist

## Pull request overview This PR changes the export pipeline to remove the single-writer-per-shard bottleneck by moving to per-range Parquet writers, enabling concurrent uploads (and effectively chunking shard output into multiple Parquet files). **Changes:** - Refactors shard export to submit scan jobs that each own a per-range writer+upload pipeline (one Parquet file per range). - Introduces range sizing logic (`computeNumRanges`) to bound per-file size via `maxObjectsPerRange`, and updates range computation accordingly. - Updates and expands unit tests to validate range computation, scan correctness across ranges, field round-tripping, pipeline shutdown behavior, and the new file naming scheme. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | usecases/export/participant.go | Switches shard job submission to per-range writer config + atomic aggregation; removes shard-wide writer pipeline. | | usecases/export/parallel_scan.go | Implements per-range writer pipeline, range sizing logic, and scanning directly into ParquetWriter per range. | | usecases/export/parallel_scan_test.go | Reworks/expands tests for range computation, scanning correctness, pipeline shutdown, and scan job execution. | | usecases/export/do_export_test.go | Updates expected output file names and adds coverage for nil-bucket and additional dataset sizes. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Collection export concurrency fixes
body:
### What's being changed:

This PR contains concurrency fixes:
  - Moved I/O out of locks: Backend initialization, JSON marshalling, and logging are no longer done while holding the participant mutex, reducing lock contention for Abort/IsRunning
  callers
  - Cancel vs. success race: Added first-caller-wins cancellation logic in the scheduler (activeExport ctx+cancel bundle) so exactly one side writes terminal metadata when Cancel and
  export completion race
  - Sibling failure detection: Node status is correctly set to Failed when a sibling node fails, using a new SetNodeError method
  - Stale request detection: Added a pointer identity check in Commit to detect Abort+re-Prepare races during backend init
  - Clearer error messages: Distinct error when backend init was never attempted vs. when it failed


None of these were real correctness issues but reduce lock contention and some edge cases

### Review checklist

## Pull request overview This PR aims to address concurrency issues in the collection export flow by reducing lock contention during status serialization and by restructuring participant commit/abort behavior. **Changes:** - Add `NodeStatus.snapshot()` to deep-copy status under lock and marshal outside the lock. - Update the participant status writer to marshal from a snapshot instead of holding the mutex during `json.Marshal`. - Rework `Participant.Commit()` to initialize the backend outside the participant mutex; adjust `Abort()` lock/logging flow. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `usecases/export/types.go` | Adds `NodeStatus.snapshot()` deep-copy helper to enable safe marshaling outside locks. | | `usecases/export/types_test.go` | Adds unit tests validating snapshot correctness and deep-copy behavior. | | `usecases/export/participant.go` | Uses snapshot in status writer; restructures `Commit()` and `Abort()` locking behavior for concurrency. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Export collection: Better marshalling
body:
### What's being changed:

This branch contains two changes
- actually run the collection export tests in the CI 🤦 
- speed up the object marshalling

Before we were doing a full FromBinary round-trip: json.Unmarshal / enrichSchemaTypes / json.Marshal of properties, intermediate []float32 allocations forvectors, and models.Object construction.

Now we're reusing the bytes we got from disk where possible. Benchmark shows ~27% faster times with 87% less allocations.

### Review checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:

title: Fix two potential issues in hidden character linter
body:
### What's being changed:

Made a commit but forgot to push it 🙈 

Fixes these issues:
  1. +++ bypass — The filter $line !~ /^\+\+\+/ skipped any added line whose content started with +++, allowing
  hidden characters on such lines to evade scanning. Fixed by narrowing the exclusion to only actual diff
  headers: +++ b/..., +++ /dev/null, or +++  (end of line).
  2. Annotation injection via filenames — The ::error file=... GitHub Actions annotation used attacker-controlled
   $file (from the diff) without escaping. A crafted filename containing %0A could inject additional workflow
  commands. Fixed by adding escape_property() and escape_message() functions that escape %, \r, \n, :, , per
  GitHub's workflow command spec.
  3. Missing pull-requests: read permission — The job only had contents: read, but gh pr diff calls the Pull
  Requests API which requires pull-requests: read. Added the permission and updated the stale comment in the
  security header.

### Review checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:
- [ ] All new code is covered by tests where it is reasonable.
- [ ] Performance tests have been run or not necessary.

-->

## Pull request overview This PR hardens the hidden/unicode character linter and its CI workflow to prevent scanning bypasses and GitHub Actions annotation injection when processing PR diffs. **Changes:** - Add Git ref validation before running the Perl diff parser and stop suppressing diff command stderr. - Escape GitHub Actions annotation properties/messages to prevent workflow command injection via filenames. - Grant `pull-requests: read` permission to the PR security lint workflow (needed for `gh pr diff`) and update the security header comment. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tools/linter_hidden_unicode.sh | Adds ref validation, safer GitHub Actions annotations, and narrows diff-header exclusion logic while scanning added lines. | | .github/workflows/pr-security-lint.yaml | Adds `pull-requests: read` permission and updates the security rationale comment accordingly. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Add linter + workflow for hidden characters in PR
body:
### What's being changed:

This adds a pipeline to check for hidden characters and obsfuscation in PRs.

The workflow runs as `pull_request_target` (potentially dangerous) so an attacker cannot change the detection script. The script NEVER checks out or runs anything that is attacker controlled.

Note that the PR does not run here, because it takes the pipelines from the basebranch which does not contain the check yet.

## Pull request overview Adds a CI guardrail to detect hidden/inperceptible Unicode characters in PR diffs (e.g., bidi overrides, zero-width characters) to mitigate trojan-source style attacks and reviewer confusion. **Changes:** - Introduces a diff-parsing linter script (`tools/linter_hidden_unicode.sh`) that scans added lines for suspicious Unicode codepoints. - Adds a dedicated `pull_request_target` GitHub Actions workflow that fetches the PR diff via the GitHub API and pipes it to the linter (without checking out PR code). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tools/linter_hidden_unicode.sh | New linter to scan unified diffs for hidden Unicode characters and optionally emit GitHub Actions annotations. | | .github/workflows/pr-security-lint.yaml | New PR-targeted workflow to run the linter securely against PR diffs. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: queue: transfer metrics ownership to scheduler
body:
### What's being changed:

Some metrics like the total number of queues and the total number of queues paused are global metrics by nature, not shard specific metrics.
This PR transfers ownership of those metrics to the scheduler, which is global to the node.

It also changes the way we collect those metrics, from incr/decr to a full count. This prevents negative counters when we restart the server.

### Review checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:

Fix OIDC SkipTLSVerify wiring and clean up related code

Wire OIDCSkipTLSVerify into runtime config in configure_api.go — it was defined but never synced, causing dynamic config updates to use a stale value
Remove dead useCertificate() wrapper (private method with no external callers); update tests to call buildHTTPClient() directly
Warn when both Certificate and SkipTLSVerify are set simultaneously, since RootCAs is effectively ignored when InsecureSkipVerify=true
Document the loadCertPool limitation: HTTP cert URL fetches use the default http.Client and will fail if the cert URL is behind a self-signed endpoint
Rename OIDC struct yaml/json tags from skip_tls_verify to insecure_skip_tls_verify to match the security-signal naming convention
Change env var from INSECURE_SKIP_OIDC_TLS_VERIFY to AUTHENTICATION_OIDC_INSECURE_SKIP_TLS_VERIFY
make yaml config same with env var name
Make certificate and insecure_skip_tls_verify mutually exclusive with test

Restore log line and fix test helper after buildHTTPClient refactor

- Restore the "configured OIDC client with custom certificate" log line
  in buildHTTPClient() that was lost during the refactor
- Update loadCertPool godoc to reflect that Certificate and SkipTLSVerify
  are now mutually exclusive (the stale chicken-and-egg caveat no longer applies)
- Fix verifyLogs test helper in Test_Middleware_CertificateDownload to search
  for the specific "custom certificate is valid" entry rather than asserting
  every log entry matches, which broke once buildHTTPClient also emits a log

feat: add on-demand query profiling support (#10696)

* feat: add on-demand query profiling support

   Add a  flag to the gRPC MetadataRequest that returns per-shard
   timing breakdowns (filter, vector search, object retrieval, sort, rescore)
   as part of SearchReply. Reuses existing SlowQueryDetails instrumentation
   so there is zero overhead when profiling is not requested.

* feat: add GraphQL support, simplify profile output, fix context propagation

- Add profile field to GraphQL _additional (returns JSON string)
- Simplify ShardProfile to name + details map (remove typed Ns fields)
- Add total_took timing per shard
- Filter out duplicate _string suffix keys and is_coordinator noise
- Fix context propagation: init ProfileCollector in Search/VectorSearch
- Use strconv instead of json.Marshal for simple types on hot path
- Mark unused proto fields as reserved
- Add godoc to all exported symbols
- JSON-serialize complex detail values (e.g. bitmap info)

* cleanup: simplify ShardProfile proto, improve godoc and test coverage

- Remove unused reserved fields 2-8 from ShardProfile proto (never released),
  renumber details to field 2
- Add field-level godoc on ShardProfile, ExtractProfiles, AttachProfileToResults,
  and Properties.Profile
- Add missing test cases: type conversions (string, int32, int64, float64,
  json.Marshal fallback), is_coordinator filtering, _string suffix without
  base key, fmt.Sprint fallback for unmarshalable types, ExtractProfiles
  copy semantics
- Fix gofumpt formatting in test file

* feat: add profile support for hybrid search

Make InitProfileCollector idempotent so hybrid search can initialize a
single shared collector before dispatching parallel sub-searches. Both
dense (vector) and sparse (BM25) sub-searches write to the same
collector via the shared context. Add profiling init to
SparseObjectSearch for the standalone BM25 path. Attach profile data
to final hybrid results after combination and pagination.

* refactor: group profile output by shard with search type sub-sections

Restructure ShardProfile to contain a map of search types (vector,
keyword, object) instead of a flat details map. For hybrid queries,
each shard now appears once with both vector and keyword sub-sections
grouped together, making the output easier to parse and consume.

Update proto (ShardProfile.searches = map<string, SearchProfile>),
gRPC reply mapping, collector internals, tests, and demo app.

* fix: bounds check in wire format, conditional time.Now(), add node name to profile

- Add bounds check in searchResultsPayload.Unmarshal to prevent panic on
  malformed/truncated profile data from remote nodes
- Gate time.Now() behind profile flag on all coordinator and incoming
  search paths to avoid overhead on non-profiled queries
- Add node name to ShardProfile so consumers can see which node served
  each shard search (proto, wire format, gRPC reply, collector)
- Add wire format roundtrip, backward compat, and malformed data tests
- Add concurrent local+remote profile collector test
- Add proto comments for QueryProfile, ShardProfile, SearchProfile
- Remove tools/dev/profile_demo (local testing utility)

* test: add acceptance tests for query profiling

Testcontainer-based tests covering:
- Vector search returns profile with shard name, node name, timing details
- BM25 keyword search returns keyword profile
- Hybrid search returns both vector and keyword profiles per shard
- Profile absent when not requested (no overhead)
- Multi-node: profiles include distinct node names across 3-node cluster
- Filtered vector search includes filter timing details

* fix: copy details map in ExtractProfiles to prevent caller mutation of collector state

Pre-allocate order slice and add test for details map independence.

* chore: add godoc and test for review findings

- Add godoc for extractQueryProfile, additionalProfileField,
  ReplicasSearchResult.Profiles
- Add test for AddRemoteProfiles with hybrid data (multiple search
  types per shard)

* Review comments

* Review comments

* Review comments

* Review comments

* Review comments

title: feat: add on-demand query profiling support
body:
### What's being changed

Adds on-demand query profiling to gRPC and GraphQL search APIs. Set `profile: true` in `MetadataRequest` (gRPC) or request `_additional { profile }` (GraphQL) to get per-shard timing breakdowns without configuring slow query logging.

**How it works:**
- A context-based `ProfileCollector` captures per-shard timing from existing slow query instrumentation — no new instrumentation needed
- Results are grouped by shard, with sub-sections per search type (e.g. `vector` and `keyword` for hybrid)
- Each shard profile includes the **node name** that executed the search, making it easy to trace which shard ran on which node in multi-node clusters
- When `profile=false` (default): zero overhead, no code paths touched

**Supported search types:** vector search (nearVector/nearObject/nearText), vector + filters, BM25 keyword, hybrid

**Available metrics** (populated from existing slow query instrumentation):

| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `total_took` | Total shard search time |
| `vector_search_took` | Vector index search time |
| `knn_search_layer_N_took` | Per-layer HNSW traversal time |
| `knn_search_rescore_took` | PQ/BQ/SQ rescore time |
| `hnsw_flat_search` | Whether flat search was used instead of HNSW |
| `filters_build_allow_list_took` | Filter allow list build time |
| `filters_ids_matched` | Number of IDs matching the filter |
| `objects_took` | Object retrieval time |
| `sort_took` | Sort time |
| `kwd_method` | BM25 method used |
| `kwd_time` | Total BM25 time |
| `kwd_1_tok_time` | BM25 tokenization time |
| `kwd_3_term_time` | BM25 term lookup time |
| `kwd_4_bmw_time` | BM25 BlockMaxWAND scoring time |

Which metrics appear depends on the query type and index state.

**Example: Filtered hybrid search across 3 nodes (gRPC)**
```
Shard: shard_abc  (node: weaviate-0)
  [keyword]
    kwd_method                             blockmaxwand
    kwd_time                               242.75µs
    kwd_1_tok_time                         18.291µs
    kwd_3_term_time                        52.083µs
    kwd_4_bmw_time                         156.417µs
    total_took                             248.833µs
  [vector]
    filters_build_allow_list_took          31.125µs
    filters_ids_matched                    847
    knn_search_layer_0_took                14µs
    objects_took                           153.542µs
    total_took                             198.666µs
    vector_search_took                     40.959µs

Shard: shard_def  (node: weaviate-1)
  [keyword]
    kwd_method                             blockmaxwand
    kwd_time                               189.333µs
    kwd_1_tok_time                         15.708µs
    kwd_3_term_time                        41.917µs
    kwd_4_bmw_time                         118.5µs
    total_took                             195.25µs
  [vector]
    filters_build_allow_list_took          27.458µs
    filters_ids_matched                    912
    knn_search_layer_0_took                11.208µs
    objects_took                           128.375µs
    total_took                             172.417µs
    vector_search_took                     35.75µs

Shard: shard_ghi  (node: weaviate-2)
  [keyword]
    kwd_method                             blockmaxwand
    kwd_time                               214.083µs
    kwd_1_tok_time                         16.542µs
    kwd_3_term_time                        47.125µs
    kwd_4_bmw_time                         134.292µs
    total_took                             220.917µs
  [vector]
    filters_build_allow_list_took          29.792µs
    filters_ids_matched                    793
    knn_search_layer_0_took                12.583µs
    objects_took                           141.208µs
    total_took                             185.333µs
    vector_search_took                     38.125µs
```

**Compatibility:**
- All proto changes are additive — safe for rolling deployments
- Old nodes ignore `profile`, new nodes handle missing profile data gracefully
- Remote shard searches include profile data with node name, so cross-node profiling works

### Review checklist

- [x] Documentation has been updated, if necessary.
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary.
- [x] All new code is covered by tests where it is reasonable.
- [ ] Performance tests have been run or not necessary.

title: feat: add tokenizer endpoint and middleware integration
body:
### What's being changed:

Add two new REST API endpoints for debugging and inspecting tokenization behavior:

#### POST /v1/tokenize — Generic tokenization
Tokenizes arbitrary text with an explicitly specified tokenizer and optional analyzer config (stopwords). Returns both indexed tokens (all raw tokens as they would be stored) and query tokens (tokens after stopword filtering, as used during search).

**Request**:
```
{
  "text": "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog",
  "tokenization": "word",
  "analyzerConfig": {
    "stopwords": {
      "preset": "en"
    }
  }
}
```

**Response**:
```
{
  "tokenization": "word",
  "analyzerConfig": { "stopwords": { "preset": "en" } },
  "indexed": ["the", "quick", "brown", "fox", "jumps", "over", "the", "lazy", "dog"],
  "query": ["quick", "brown", "fox", "jumps", "lazy", "dog"]
}
```

#### POST /v1/schema/{className}/properties/{propertyName}/tokenize — Property-specific tokenization
Tokenizes text using the tokenization type and stopwords config already configured on a collection's property. No need to specify the tokenizer — it's resolved from the schema.

**Request**:

```
{
  "text": "The café serves crème brûlée"
}
```

**Response**:
```
{
  "tokenization": "word",
  "indexed": ["the", "café", "serves", "crème", "brûlée"],
  "query": ["café", "serves", "crème", "brûlée"]
}
```

#### Supported tokenizers
All tokenizers are supported: word, lowercase, whitespace, field, trigram, and the optional APAC tokenizers (gse, gse_ch, kagome_kr, kagome_ja) when enabled.

#### Implementation details

Endpoints are defined in the OpenAPI spec and wired into the swagger-generated API via `setupTokenizeHandlers` in `configure_api.go`, following the same pattern as all other REST handlers in the codebase.
The property-specific endpoint reads the class schema via `SchemaManager.ReadOnlyClass()` to resolve the property's tokenization type and the class's stopwords config.
The indexed vs query distinction mirrors what actually happens during indexing and search: all tokens are indexed, but stopwords are filtered out at query time.


#### Files changed

- adapters/handlers/rest/handlers_tokenize.go — Handler logic for both endpoints
- adapters/handlers/rest/handlers_tokenize_test.go — Tests covering all tokenizer types including GSE and Kagome
- adapters/handlers/rest/middlewares.go — Wire the middleware into the HTTP chain

#### Test plan

-  Unit tests for generic tokenization with all basic tokenizers (word, lowercase, whitespace, field, trigram)
-  Unit tests for stopword filtering (english preset, no preset, custom additions, removals)
-  Unit tests for GSE tokenization (Japanese and Chinese)
-  Unit tests for Kagome tokenization (Korean and Japanese)
-  Unit tests for middleware routing (correct paths handled, non-matching paths fall through)
-  Unit tests for error cases (missing text, missing tokenization, unknown tokenization, invalid stopwords)
-  Manual test with a running Weaviate instance against both endpoints

### Review checklist

- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:
- [ ] All new code is covered by tests where it is reasonable.
- [ ] Performance tests have been run or not necessary.

title: feat: add accent insensitive processing options for text properties
body:
## What's being changed

- Introduced TextAnalyserConfig model to handle accent folding settings.
- Updated Property and NestedProperty models to include processing options.
- Enhanced Analyzer methods to support accent insensitive tokenization.
- Modified BM25Searcher to utilize accent folding in query processing.
- Added tests for accent folding functionality in tokenizer.
- Updated OpenAPI schema to reflect new processing options.

## Common Latin diacritics folded

Character | Folded to | Language examples
-- | -- | --
à á â ã ä å | a | French, Portuguese, German, Swedish
è é ê ë | e | French, Portuguese, Spanish
ì í î ï | i | Italian, French
ò ó ô õ ö | o | Portuguese, German, Swedish
ù ú û ü | u | French, German, Spanish
ý ÿ | y | French, Icelandic
ñ | n | Spanish
ç | c | French, Portuguese, Turkish
ş | s | Turkish, Romanian
ţ ț | t | Romanian
ă | a | Romanian
ź ż ž | z | Polish, Czech
ś š | s | Polish, Czech
ć č | c | Polish, Czech, Croatian
ń ň | n | Polish, Czech
ř | r | Czech
ď | d | Czech
ľ ĺ ł | l | Slovak, Polish (ł via table*)
ő | o | Hungarian
ű | u | Hungarian
ā ē ī ō ū | a e i o u | Latvian, Māori
æ | ae | Danish, Norwegian (table*)
ø | o | Danish, Norwegian (table*)
ð | d | Icelandic (table*)
þ | th | Icelandic (table*)

## What NFD decomposition handles automatically

Everything with a combining mark — the vast majority of accented Latin characters (acute, grave, circumflex, tilde, dieresis, caron/háček, cedilla, ogonek, macron, breve, ring, dot above/below, etc.).

## Special cases NOT handled by NFD (*)

These are single codepoints that do not decompose. They are handled by an explicit replacement table in accent_fold.go:

Character | Language
-- | --
ł (U+0142) | Polish L-stroke
æ (U+00E6) | ligature
ø (U+00F8) | O-stroke
ð (U+00F0) | eth
þ (U+00FE) | thorn
đ (U+0111) | D-stroke (Croatian/Vietnamese)
ß (U+00DF) | German sharp s

The implementation does NFD + strip Mn (nonspacing marks), which covers ~95% of real-world Latin-language diacritics. The explicit table covers the remaining stroked letters, ligatures, special letters, and hooked/tailed letters.

### TODO:

- [x] which chars to fold
- [x] if it applies to filterable
- [x] downgrade path
- [x] naming the properties

title: Concurrent collection export
body:
### What's being changed:


We start N worker goroutines and then iterate through the shards to submit jobs. A shard is divided into ranges which are submitted to the workers (similar to the parallel vector index initialization). After all ranges of a shard are processed we tear the shard down.

Goals are:
- maximizing speed/minimizing export time
- minimize the time a shard is held, by submitting all jobs for a shard in order
- fail fast. As soon as one job failed, the complete export will fail

### Review checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:
- [ ] All new code is covered by tests where it is reasonable.
- [ ] Performance tests have been run or not necessary.

reviews.body: ### Orca Security Scan Summary | Status | Check | Issues by priority | | | ------- | ----- | ------------------ | - | | Passed | Infrastructure as Code | 0   0   0   0 | View in Orca | | Passed | SAST | 0   0   0   0 |

## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a concurrent (“N-worker pool”) export implementation by splitting shard scans into key ranges and processing them in parallel, while streaming results into a single Parquet writer per shard. It also refactors some shared export utilities and adds tests for the new parallel scan/export paths.

**Changes:**
- Add a parallel range-scanning worker pool and per-shard writer/upload pipeline to speed up exports.
- Extend `ParquetWriter` with `WriteRow`, switch vector serialization to `byteops`, and make object counting concurrency-safe.
- Add new unit tests covering parallel scanning and `doExport`, and tighten `NodeStatus` shard progress initialization.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| usecases/export/types.go | Lazily initializes shard progress maps/entries when updating progress. |
| usecases/export/scheduler.go | Removes the old shared `exportShardData` helper (now replaced by parallel scan path). |
| usecases/export/participant.go | Implements worker pool, range submission, and shard writer/upload pipeline. |
| usecases/export/parquet_writer.go | Adds `WriteRow`, switches to atomic counter, and uses `byteops` for vector serialization. |
| usecases/export/parallel_scan.go | New range computation + range scanning logic used by workers. |
| usecases/export/parallel_scan_test.go | New tests validating parallel scan correctness and range computation. |
| usecases/export/do_export_test.go | New tests validating end-to-end `doExport` behavior with the new pipeline. |
| entities/errors/error_group_wrapper.go | Makes routine counter atomic and adds `TryGo` wrapper with panic recovery. |
</details>






---

## Pull request overview This PR improves multi-node export cancellation/status robustness by persisting more accurate terminal metadata states and adding retry behavior to metadata writes, reducing the chance of “stuck” or misleading export states. **Changes:** - Promote assembled multi-node terminal status (SUCCESS/FAILED) into the metadata file. - Persist CANCELED metadata even when best-effort aborts cannot reach all nodes, and reflect that in the stored error message. - Add retries to metadata writes to reduce broken/partial states. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | usecases/export/scheduler.go | Promotes assembled terminal status to metadata, changes cancel behavior to persist CANCELED despite abort errors, and adds retry logic to metadata writes. | | usecases/export/scheduler_test.go | Adds a test asserting that terminal multi-node status gets promoted into the metadata file. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix: Add IPv6 support for clustering (#10722)
body:
* fix: Add IPv6 support for clustering

Replace fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", host, port) with net.JoinHostPort() and strings.Split(addr, ":")[0] with net.SplitHostPort() across all clustering code. Enables Weaviate clustering on IPv6-only networks.

13 fixes across 8 files. Zero behavior change for IPv4.

* Add IPv6 test coverage for resolvers and fix godoc placement

- Add IPv6 test cases for raft ServerAddr (bracket notation, local cluster)
- Add IPv6 test cases for RPC Address resolver
- Fix misplaced NodeAddress godoc that was attached to extractJoinHost
- Fix zone ID test case: net.SplitHostPort preserves %25 encoding



* fix: Rename extractJoinHost to extractHost and deduplicate NodeAddress

Rename extractJoinHost to extractHost since it handles both join and node addresses. Deduplicate NodeAddress by reusing extractHost instead of inlining the same net.SplitHostPort logic.


Backport: https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/pull/10722


---------

title: Fix race in RBAC restore
body:
### What's being changed:

The [previous fix](https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/pull/10706) was incomplete and would still allow a very short time window in which stale permissions could stay in the cache for longer:

```
  1. Restore starts — clears policies, loads new ones from snapshot, calls InvalidateCache()
  2. A concurrent Enforce call starts before InvalidateCache(), evaluates against the old (stale) model,
  gets true
  3. InvalidateCache() completes — cache is now empty
  4. The concurrent Enforce finishes and writes its stale true result into the cache
  5. All subsequent Enforce calls hit the cache and get the stale true
 ```

I benchmarked adding the Rlock into Enforce and it only added `5-10ns`. As Restore is rarely used outside of startup it should have a neglible effect

e2e: https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-e2e-tests/actions/runs/22989609561
chaos: https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-chaos-engineering/actions/runs/22989611959

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:

## Pull request overview This PR tightens RBAC restore semantics by preventing concurrent authorization checks from re-populating Casbin’s enforce cache with stale decisions during `Manager.Restore()`, closing a small race window described in the PR. **Changes:** - Introduces a `restoreLock` (RWMutex) in the RBAC `Manager` and uses it to block concurrent permission checks during `Restore()`. - Wraps `checkPermissions()` with `RLock()` so `Restore()` can safely take a write lock and prevent stale cache writes. - Updates `TestRestoreInvalidatesEnforceCache` to exercise the race scenario via `checkPermissions()` using a real `Principal`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `usecases/auth/authorization/rbac/manager.go` | Adds `restoreLock` and uses it to serialize `Restore()` vs. permission checks/cache invalidation. | | `usecases/auth/authorization/rbac/manager_test.go` | Adjusts the restore/cache invalidation test to call `checkPermissions()` with a `Principal`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Export collection: Fix race between create and cancel
body:
### What's being changed:

For single node clusters the export-plan was uploaded in a background goroutine. This leaves a very short timewindow where the cancel request does not find the plan and returns a 404. This was fixed by moving writing the plan out of the background process so we can guarantee it was written before we return

Note that there is a test that covers this scenario that never failed, so it was veeeery unlikely to be triggered anyways: test/modules/export-filesystem/export_cancel_test.go

title: Close reader after finishing to avoid deadlock in restore
body:
### What's being changed:

The goroutine was hanging here making causing the backup to never finish:

```
goroutine 3922 [chan receive, 1 minutes]: 1 times: [[3922]
github.com/weaviate/weaviate/usecases/backup.(*fileWriter).readAndUnzipChunk(0x40009aab40, {0x4002293e40, 0x37}, {0x4002e7c1d8, 0x4}, {0x4002b70120, 0x2f}, 0x40044d1220)
	github.com/weaviate/weaviate/usecases/backup/backend.go:882 +0x4d8
github.com/weaviate/weaviate/usecases/backup.(*fileWriter).writeTempFiles.func2()
	github.com/weaviate/weaviate/usecases/backup/backend.go:792 +0xfc
github.com/weaviate/weaviate/usecases/backup.(*fileWriter).writeTempFiles.(*ErrorGroupWrapper).Go.func5()
	github.com/weaviate/weaviate/entities/errors/error_group_wrapper.go:101 +0x80
golang.org/x/sync/errgroup.(*Group).Go.func1()
	golang.org/x/sync@v0.19.0/errgroup/errgroup.go:93 +0x4c
created by golang.org/x/sync/errgroup.(*Group).Go in goroutine 3328
	golang.org/x/sync@v0.19.0/errgroup/errgroup.go:78 +0x90
```

The underlying issue was:
- the writer writes chunks of 32kb into the pipe
- the EOF might be in the middle of the chunk
- the reader exits after seeing the EOF
- there is data left in the pipe and the writer blocks <= this is solved by closing

### Review checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been updated, if necessary. Link to changed documentation:
- [ ] Chaos pipeline run or not necessary. Link to pipeline:
- [ ] All new code is covered by tests where it is reasonable.
- [ ] Performance tests have been run or not necessary.

-->

reviews.body: ### Orca Security Scan Summary | Status | Check | Issues by priority | | | ------- | ----- | ------------------ | - | | Passed | Secrets | 0   0   0   0 | View in Orca |

## Pull request overview Fixes a potential restore deadlock when unzipping a downloaded chunk finishes early (end-of-stream) while the download goroutine is still writing trailing bytes into an `io.Pipe`. **Changes:** - Close the unzipper’s pipe reader after `ReadChunk()` to unblock in-flight writes with `io.ErrClosedPipe`, and drain the download error channel. - Treat `io.ErrClosedPipe` from the download goroutine as non-fatal after successful unzip. - Add a regression test that reproduces the trailing-bytes deadlock scenario. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | usecases/backup/backend.go | Closes the unzip pipe reader after `ReadChunk()` and drains the read goroutine error to prevent hangs/leaks. | | usecases/backup/backend_test.go | Adds a test ensuring trailing bytes after a valid gzip+tar stream do not deadlock restore/unzip. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for Google AI Studio (Gemini API) models in the `multi2vec-google` module, which previously only supported Google Vertex AI. It introduces a new `apiEndpoint` configuration property to route requests either to the Vertex AI endpoint or the Generative AI (Gemini) endpoint, with corresponding changes to payload construction, response parsing, authentication headers, and validation logic.

**Changes:**
- Added Gemini API integration with separate request/response types (`batchEmbedContents`, `batchEmbedResponse`), payload builder (`getGeminiPayload`), and response parser (`getEmbeddingsFromGeminiResponse`), using the `apiEndpoint` config to switch between Vertex AI and Gemini code paths.
- Changed `Dimensions` and `VideoIntervalSeconds` from value types (`int64`) to pointer types (`*int64`) to support optional dimensions for non-legacy models, and introduced `isLegacyModel()` to conditionally apply validation only to the `multimodalembedding@001` model.
- Refactored integration tests to be parameterized via `testCase` struct, supporting conditional video tests and different model/endpoint configurations.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

## Pull request overview

This PR implements support for splitting large backup files across multiple chunks when they exceed a configurable size threshold. The implementation adds a new environment variable `BACKUP_SPLIT_FILE_SIZE` (default 50GB) that controls when files should be split across backup chunks, complementing the existing `BACKUP_CHUNK_TARGET_SIZE` (default 1GB) that controls overall chunk sizes.

**Changes:**
- Added split file mechanism using TAR PAX records to track file offsets during backup and restore
- Modified backup/restore logic to handle files larger than the split threshold by writing them in parts across multiple chunks
- Added configuration for split file size with environment variable `BACKUP_SPLIT_FILE_SIZE`

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| usecases/config/config_handler.go | Added `DefaultBackupSplitFileSize` constant (50GB) and `SplitFileSize` field to Backup struct |
| usecases/config/environment.go | Added parsing for `BACKUP_SPLIT_FILE_SIZE` environment variable |
| usecases/backup/zip.go | Implemented split file logic: new `SplitFile` type, `WriteSplitFile` method, PAX record handling in `copyFile`, and modified signatures for `NewZip`, `WriteShard`, `WriteRegulars`, and `WriteRegular` |
| usecases/backup/backend.go | Updated compress function to handle split files in the backup loop, fixed typo from `chuckShards` to `chunkShards` |
| usecases/backup/zip_test.go | Added comprehensive tests for split file scenarios including round-trip, boundary cases, threshold behavior, and PAX record handling |
| adapters/repos/db/backup_integration_test.go | Added end-to-end integration test with real database to verify split file backup and restore |
</details>






---

💡 <a href="/weaviate/weaviate/new/main?filename=.github/instructions/*.instructions.md"

commits.message:
Add file splitting

Rebase of 771eaae0ca onto stable/v1.34, combining file splitting
support with the FileList abstraction, CloseWithError pattern, and
configurable chunk target size from HEAD.
Add split size for large chunks with a default of 50GB
Fix splitting for the first file in a chunk
More tests for split file restore
Fix bug when first regular file was above threshold
Better error returns for backup restore
Add better error logging for tests
Add integrattion tests for file splitting
Better names and validation that splitFileSize>bigFileThreshold
Write splitfile on detection and add to incremental backup info
Adapt test to recent code changes

## Pull request overview

This PR adds plumbing to capture the `X-Weaviate-Client` identifier (SDK + version) across both HTTP and gRPC requests and includes it in RBAC audit logging, while also refactoring client header parsing into a reusable helper.

**Changes:**
- Refactors client identification into `telemetry.IdentifyClientFromHeader` and adds unit tests for parsing.
- Adds HTTP middleware and a gRPC interceptor to propagate client header info into request context for audit logging.
- Wires the telemetry client tracker into gRPC server creation so gRPC requests can be tracked as well.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| usecases/telemetry/client_tracker_test.go | Adds tests for parsing `X-Weaviate-Client` header values. |
| usecases/telemetry/client_tracker.go | Extracts header parsing into `IdentifyClientFromHeader`. |
| usecases/telemetry/client_middleware.go | Adds gRPC unary interceptor + header-based tracking helper for client telemetry. |
| usecases/auth/authorization/rbac/authorizer.go | Adds `client_version` field to RBAC audit logs (from context). |
| adapters/handlers/rest/middlewares.go | Adds new REST middleware to inject client header into context. |
| adapters/handlers/rest/grpc.go | Extends gRPC server creation to accept a telemetry client tracker. |
| adapters/handlers/rest/configure_api.go | Creates telemeter before gRPC server to pass its client tracker into gRPC server creation. |
| adapters/handlers/rest/client_version_middleware_test.go | Adds tests for REST client-version context injection middleware. |
| adapters/handlers/rest/client_version_middleware.go | Implements REST middleware to store `X-Weaviate-Client` in context. |
| adapters/handlers/grpc/server.go | Adds gRPC interceptor to store `x-weaviate-client` in context and optionally tracks clie
